using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;
using MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Auth;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure;

public static class DependencyInjection
{
    public static IServiceCollection AddInfrastructure(this IServiceCollection services)
    {
        services.AddScoped<IAuthService, AuthService>();

        return services;
    }
}

src\MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure\Services\Auth\AuthService.cs
========================================================================================================================
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Models.Auth;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Auth;

public sealed class AuthService : IAuthService
{
    public Task<LoginResult> LoginAsync(string username, string password)
    {
        username = (username ?? string.Empty).Trim();
        password = password ?? string.Empty;

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(username) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(password))
        {
            return Task.FromResult(new LoginResult
            {
                Success = false,
                Message = "نام کاربری و رمز عبور الزامی است."
            });
        }

        // Temporary roadmap-safe development login.
        // Later this should be replaced by database-backed authentication.
        if (string.Equals(username, "admin", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
            password == "admin123")
        {
            return Task.FromResult(new LoginResult
            {
                Success = true,
                Username = "admin",
                DisplayName = "مدیر سیستم",
                Message = "ورود موفقیت‌آمیز بود."
            });
        }

        return Task.FromResult(new LoginResult
        {
            Success = false,
            Message = "نام کاربری یا رمز عبور اشتباه است."
        });
    }
}

using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Models.Auth;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Auth;

[Obsolete("SimpleAuthService is deprecated. Use AuthService instead.")]
public sealed class SimpleAuthService : IAuthService
{
    public Task<LoginResult> LoginAsync(string username, string password)
    {
        return Task.FromResult(new LoginResult
        {
            Success = false,
            Message = "SimpleAuthService is deprecated."
        });

}

using BCrypt.Net;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Security;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Security
{
    public class BCryptPasswordHasher : IPasswordHasher
    {
        public string Hash(string password)
        {
            return BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.HashPassword(password);
        }

        public bool Verify(string password, string hash)
        {
            return BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.Verify(password, hash);
        }
    }
}

src\MyCompanyApp.Persistence\AppDbContext.cs
========================================================================================================================
using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Common.Interfaces;
using MyCompanyApp.Domain.Entities;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Persistence;

public class AppDbContext : DbContext, IAppDbContext
{
    public AppDbContext(DbContextOptions<AppDbContext> options) : base(options)
    {
    }

    public DbSet<User> Users => Set<User>();
    public DbSet<Role> Roles => Set<Role>();
    public DbSet<Permission> Permissions => Set<Permission>();
    public DbSet<UserRole> UserRoles => Set<UserRole>();
    public DbSet<RolePermission> RolePermissions => Set<RolePermission>();

    protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    {
        base.OnModelCreating(modelBuilder);

        modelBuilder.Entity<User>(entity =>
        {
            entity.HasKey(user => user.Id);
            entity.Property(user => user.Username)
                .IsRequired()
                .HasMaxLength(100);

            entity.HasIndex(user => user.Username)
                .IsUnique();

            entity.Property(user => user.PasswordHash)
                .IsRequired();

            entity.Property(user => user.IsActive)
                .IsRequired();
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<Role>(entity =>
        {
            entity.HasKey(role => role.Id);
            entity.Property(role => role.Name)
                .IsRequired()
                .HasMaxLength(100);

            entity.HasIndex(role => role.Name)
                .IsUnique();
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<Permission>(entity =>
        {
            entity.HasKey(permission => permission.Id);
            entity.Property(permission => permission.Code)
                .IsRequired()
                .HasMaxLength(150);

            entity.HasIndex(permission => permission.Code)
                .IsUnique();

            entity.Property(permission => permission.Description)
                .HasMaxLength(500);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<UserRole>(entity =>
        {
            entity.HasKey(userRole => new { userRole.UserId, userRole.RoleId });

            entity.HasOne(userRole => userRole.User)
                .WithMany(user => user.UserRoles)
                .HasForeignKey(userRole => userRole.UserId)
                .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade);

            entity.HasOne(userRole => userRole.Role)
                .WithMany()
                .HasForeignKey(userRole => userRole.RoleId)
                .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<RolePermission>(entity =>
        {
            entity.HasKey(rolePermission => new { rolePermission.RoleId, rolePermission.PermissionId });

            entity.HasOne(rolePermission => rolePermission.Role)
                .WithMany(role => role.RolePermissions)
                .HasForeignKey(rolePermission => rolePermission.RoleId)
                .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade);

            entity.HasOne(rolePermission => rolePermission.Permission)
                .WithMany()
                .HasForeignKey(rolePermission => rolePermission.PermissionId)
                .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade);
        });
    }

    public override Task<int> SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
        => base.SaveChangesAsync(cancellationToken);
}

========================================================================================================================
using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Common.Interfaces;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Persistence;

public static class DependencyInjection
{
    public static IServiceCollection AddPersistence(this IServiceCollection services, string connectionString)
    {
        services.AddDbContext<AppDbContext>(options =>
            options.UseSqlite(connectionString));

        services.AddScoped<IAppDbContext>(provider =>
            provider.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>());

        return services;
    }
}

src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\App.xaml.cs
========================================================================================================================
using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private ServiceProvider? _serviceProvider;

    protected override void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        var services = new ServiceCollection();
        ConfigureServices(services);

        _serviceProvider = services.BuildServiceProvider();

        var loginWindow = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<LoginWindow>();
        var loginResult = loginWindow.ShowDialog();

        if (loginResult == true)
        {
            var mainWindow = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<MainWindow>();
            MainWindow = mainWindow;
            mainWindow.Show();
        }
        else
        {
            Shutdown();
        }
    }

    private static void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
    {
        services.AddInfrastructure();

        services.AddTransient<LoginWindow>();
        services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();
    }

    protected override void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        _serviceProvider?.Dispose();
        base.OnExit(e);
    }
}

using BCrypt.Net;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Contracts.Security;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Application.Services.Security;

public class BCryptPasswordHasher : IPasswordHasher
{
    public string Hash(string password)
    {
        return BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.HashPassword(password);
    }

    public bool Verify(string password, string passwordHash)
    {
        return BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.Verify(password, passwordHash);
    }
}

src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\Infrastructure\Persistence\AppDbContext.cs
========================================================================================================================
using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using MyCompanyApp.Domain.Entities.Identity;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Persistence;

public class AppDbContext : DbContext
{
    public AppDbContext(DbContextOptions<AppDbContext> options) : base(options) { }

    public DbSet<User> Users => Set<User>();
    public DbSet<Role> Roles => Set<Role>();
    public DbSet<Permission> Permissions => Set<Permission>();
    public DbSet<UserRole> UserRoles => Set<UserRole>();
    public DbSet<RolePermission> RolePermissions => Set<RolePermission>();

    protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    {
        modelBuilder.Entity<User>(b =>
        {
            b.HasIndex(x => x.Username).IsUnique();
            b.Property(x => x.Username).HasMaxLength(100).IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.PasswordHash).HasMaxLength(500).IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.FullName).HasMaxLength(200).IsRequired();
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<Role>(b =>
        {
            b.HasIndex(x => x.Name).IsUnique();
            b.Property(x => x.Name).HasMaxLength(50).IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.Description).HasMaxLength(250);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<Permission>(b =>
        {
            b.HasIndex(x => x.Key).IsUnique();
            b.Property(x => x.Key).HasMaxLength(150).IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.DisplayName).HasMaxLength(200).IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.ModuleName).HasMaxLength(100);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<UserRole>(b =>
        {
            b.HasIndex(x => new { x.UserId, x.RoleId }).IsUnique();
            b.HasOne(x => x.User).WithMany(x => x.UserRoles).HasForeignKey(x => x.UserId);
            b.HasOne(x => x.Role).WithMany(x => x.UserRoles).HasForeignKey(x => x.RoleId);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<RolePermission>(b =>
        {
            b.HasIndex(x => new { x.RoleId, x.PermissionId }).IsUnique();
            b.HasOne(x => x.Role).WithMany(x => x.RolePermissions).HasForeignKey(x => x.RoleId);
            b.HasOne(x => x.Permission).WithMany().HasForeignKey(x => x.PermissionId);
        });

        modelBuilder.Entity<Role>().HasData(
            new Role { Id = Guid.Parse("11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"), Name = "ADMIN", Description = "Full system access" },
            new Role { Id = Guid.Parse("22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222"), Name = "MANAGER", Description = "Operational management" },
            new Role { Id = Guid.Parse("33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333"), Name = "OPERATOR", Description = "Daily operations" },
            new Role { Id = Guid.Parse("44444444-4444-4444-4444-444444444444"), Name = "VIEWER", Description = "Read only access" }
        );

        modelBuilder.Entity<Permission>().HasData(
            new Permission { Id = Guid.Parse("aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa"), Key = "system.bootstrap", DisplayName = "Bootstrap system", ModuleName = "Identity" },
            new Permission { Id = Guid.Parse("bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb"), Key = "users.manage", DisplayName = "Manage users", ModuleName = "Identity" },
            new Permission { Id = Guid.Parse("cccccccc-cccc-cccc-cccc-cccccccccccc"), Key = "roles.manage", DisplayName = "Manage roles", ModuleName = "Identity" },
            new Permission { Id = Guid.Parse("dddddddd-dddd-dddd-dddd-dddddddddddd"), Key = "permissions.manage", DisplayName = "Manage permissions", ModuleName = "Identity" }
        );
    }
}

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.LoginWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="ورود به سیستم"
        Height="430"
        Width="520"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        ResizeMode="NoResize"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft"
        Background="#F3F4F6">
    <Grid>
        <Border Width="390"
                Padding="28"
                Background="White"
                CornerRadius="18"
                VerticalAlignment="Center"
                HorizontalAlignment="Center">
            <Border.Effect>
                <DropShadowEffect BlurRadius="24"
                                  ShadowDepth="0"
                                  Opacity="0.18"/>
            </Border.Effect>

            <StackPanel>
                <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                           FontSize="26"
                           FontWeight="Bold"
                           Foreground="#111827"
                           HorizontalAlignment="Center"
                           Margin="0,0,0,6"/>

                <TextBlock Text="ورود به پنل مدیریت"
                           FontSize="14"
                           Foreground="#6B7280"
                           HorizontalAlignment="Center"
                           Margin="0,0,0,28"/>

                <TextBlock Text="نام کاربری"
                           FontSize="13"
                           Foreground="#374151"
                           Margin="0,0,0,7"/>

                <TextBox x:Name="UsernameTextBox"
                         Height="42"
                         FontSize="15"
                         Padding="10,0"
                         VerticalContentAlignment="Center"
                         BorderBrush="#D1D5DB"
                         BorderThickness="1"
                         Text="admin"
                         Margin="0,0,0,16"/>

                <TextBlock Text="رمز عبور"
                           FontSize="13"
                           Foreground="#374151"
                           Margin="0,0,0,7"/>

                <PasswordBox x:Name="PasswordBox"
                             Height="42"
                             FontSize="15"
                             Padding="10,0"
                             VerticalContentAlignment="Center"
                             BorderBrush="#D1D5DB"
                             BorderThickness="1"
                             Password="admin123"
                             Margin="0,0,0,12"/>

                <TextBlock x:Name="ErrorTextBlock"
                           Text=""
                           Foreground="#DC2626"
                           FontSize="13"
                           TextWrapping="Wrap"
                           MinHeight="22"
                           Margin="0,0,0,12"/>

                <Button x:Name="LoginButton"
                        Content="ورود"
                        Height="44"
                        FontSize="15"
                        FontWeight="SemiBold"
                        Background="#2563EB"
                        Foreground="White"
                        BorderThickness="0"
                        Cursor="Hand"
                        Click="LoginButton_Click"/>

                <TextBlock Text="حساب تست: admin / admin123"
                           FontSize="12"
                           Foreground="#9CA3AF"
                           HorizontalAlignment="Center"
                           Margin="0,18,0,0"/>
            </StackPanel>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\LoginWindow.xaml.cs
========================================================================================================================
using System;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class LoginWindow : Window
{
    private readonly IAuthService _authService;

    public LoginWindow(IAuthService authService)
    {
        _authService = authService ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(authService));
        InitializeComponent();
    }

    public string? AuthenticatedUsername { get; private set; }

    public string? AuthenticatedDisplayName { get; private set; }

    private async void LoginButton_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
    {
        await TryLoginAsync();
    }

    private async Task TryLoginAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            SetBusy(true);
            ErrorTextBlock.Text = string.Empty;

            var username = UsernameTextBox.Text;
            var password = PasswordBox.Password;

            var result = await _authService.LoginAsync(username, password);

            if (result.Success)
            {
                AuthenticatedUsername = result.Username;
                AuthenticatedDisplayName = result.DisplayName;

                DialogResult = true;
                Close();
                return;
            }

            ErrorTextBlock.Text = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(result.Message)
                ? "ورود ناموفق بود."
                : result.Message;
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            ErrorTextBlock.Text = "خطا در فرآیند ورود: " + ex.Message;
        }
        finally
        {
            SetBusy(false);
        }
    }

    private void SetBusy(bool isBusy)
    {
        LoginButton.IsEnabled = !isBusy;
        UsernameTextBox.IsEnabled = !isBusy;
        PasswordBox.IsEnabled = !isBusy;
        LoginButton.Content = isBusy ? "در حال بررسی..." : "ورود";
    }
}

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="720"
        Width="1220"
        MinHeight="650"
        MinWidth="1050"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\MainWindow.xaml.cs
========================================================================================================================
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Models;

public class UserContext
{
    public Guid UserId { get; set; }

    public string Username { get; set; } = "";

    public string DisplayName { get; set; } = "";

    public string Role { get; set; } = "";

    public bool IsAdmin => Role == "Admin";
}


========================================================================================================================
src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\Services\Security\UserContext.cs
========================================================================================================================
namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Services.Security;

public sealed class UserContext : IUserContext
{
    public CurrentUser? CurrentUser { get; private set; }

    public bool IsAuthenticated => CurrentUser?.IsAuthenticated == true;

    public void SignIn(string userName, string displayName, string role)
    {
        CurrentUser = new CurrentUser
        {
            UserName = userName,
            DisplayName = displayName,
            Role = role,
            IsAuthenticated = true
        };
    }

    public void SignOut()
    {
        CurrentUser = null;
    }
}

## File: ARCHITECTURE.md
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_backup_20260629_160810\ARCHITECTURE.md
---
# ARCHITECTURE

Generated: 2026-06-27 14:42:16

## Baseline
The solution follows a modular layered WPF architecture with a dedicated shell project.

## Core Layers
- src/MyCompanyApp.Domain — domain model and rules
- src/MyCompanyApp.Application — use-cases and application services
- src/MyCompanyApp.Persistence — persistence and data access
- src/MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure — external/infrastructure implementations
- src/MyCompanyApp.Wpf — desktop composition root and UI shell

## Platform Libraries
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Core
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Logging
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Configuration
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Auth
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Export
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Plugin
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.PersianDate
- src/Platform/MyCompanyApp.Platform.Reminder

## Feature Modules
- src/Modules/MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard
- src/Modules/MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users
- src/Modules/MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave

## Architectural Rules
- Keep Domain independent from UI/infrastructure concerns.
- Keep Application free from WPF-specific dependencies.
- Use src/MyCompanyApp.Wpf as the composition root.
- Keep canonical scans/builds limited to the source tree.
- Avoid unnecessary refactoring until roadmap Phase 1 is verified in code.

## Phase 1 Verification Scope
- Dependency injection composition
- Configuration bootstrap
- Logging integration
- Exception handling flow
- Base/shared services
- Testability of application services

# MyCompanyApp.Wpf Development Roadmap

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:46

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 1 â€” CORE INFRASTRUCTURE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Establish strong architecture foundation.

Tasks

Setup dependency injection
Create configuration management
Create logging infrastructure
Create error handling framework
Create base services
Ensure application layer testability

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 2 â€” DATABASE & PERSISTENCE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Reliable and optimized database layer.

Tasks

Database context design
Entity configuration
Index strategy
Performance optimization
Migration system
Database health monitoring

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 3 â€” AUTHENTICATION & USER MANAGEMENT
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Secure user authentication and authorization.

Features

Professional Login Form
Password hashing
User management panel
Role-based access control (RBAC)
Permission system for application sections

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 4 â€” MODERN UI FRAMEWORK
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Create modern and attractive UI.

Features

Professional Dashboard
Custom message dialog system
Theme switching (Light / Dark / Custom)
Font switching
Reusable UI components
Smooth UI interactions

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 5 â€” DASHBOARD SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Provide quick insight into system status.

Features

Statistics widgets
Charts
Activity overview
Quick navigation panels

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 6 â€” REMINDER SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Provide internal reminder system.

Features

Time-based reminders
Notification popup
Recurring reminders
Reminder management panel

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 7 â€” LEAVE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Employee leave tracking.

Features

Leave request submission
Leave approval workflow
Leave history
Leave reporting

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 8 â€” REPORTING ENGINE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Dynamic reporting capabilities.

Features

Dynamic report builder
Data filtering
Interactive reports
Chart-based reports

Export capabilities

Excel export
PDF export
PowerPoint export for presentation reports

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 9 â€” BACKUP SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Data safety.

Features

Manual backup
Scheduled backup
Restore functionality
Backup compression

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 10 â€” PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Ensure fast application performance.

Tasks

Database query optimization
Async processing
Memory management
UI responsiveness improvements

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 11 â€” TESTING INFRASTRUCTURE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Reliable and maintainable system.

Tasks

Unit tests
Integration tests
Service testing
Data layer testing

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 12 â€” INSTALLER & DEPLOYMENT
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Smooth installation for customers.

Features

Stable installer package
Dependency verification
Clean publish output
Zero installation errors

-------------------------------------------------
FINAL PRODUCT VISION
-------------------------------------------------

A high-performance offline enterprise desktop system
with modern UI, powerful reporting, reminder system,
leave management, backup infrastructure and
professional user management.

# AI Project Context

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:43

Project Root:
G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

Project Type:
Enterprise Offline Desktop Application

Architecture:
Clean Architecture + MVVM

Core Characteristics:

- Fully Offline
- Persian Calendar Support
- Reminder Engine
- Backup System
- Logging Infrastructure
- Role-Based Access Control
- High Performance
- Custom Theming Engine
- Custom Dialog Framework
- Testable Application Layer
- Zero Installation Errors Policy

Development Direction:

Build infrastructure first.
Then UI framework.
Then user management.
Then reminder engine.
Then backup & logging hardening.
Then installer preparation.

Goal:

Production-ready, stable, maintainable system.

## ARCHITECTURE

# Architecture Baseline

Architecture Style:
Clean Architecture + MVVM

Layers

Domain
Application
Infrastructure
Persistence
Reporting
WPF

Dependency Rules

Domain -> none
Application -> Domain
Infrastructure -> Domain, Application
Persistence -> Domain, Application
Reporting -> Domain, Application
WPF -> all layers

Forbidden

Domain must not reference other layers
Application must not reference WPF

FILE: PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md
=====================================================

# PROJECT DOCUMENTATION

## Project Name
HR and Attendance Management System

## Technology Stack
- .NET 8
- WPF
- Clean Architecture
- Entity Framework Core
- SQLite
- CommunityToolkit.Mvvm
- Serilog
- ClosedXML
- QuestPDF
- LiveCharts
- OpenXML

## Main Modules
- Personnel Management
- Attendance Tracking
- User and Role Management
- Dashboard
- Reports (Excel, PDF, PowerPoint, Charts)
- Backup and Restore
- Persian Reminder System
- Theme / Language / Font Customization

## Architecture Layers
- Domain
- Application
- Infrastructure
- Persistence
- Reporting
- WPF UI

## Current Status
Initial enterprise-grade project skeleton has been created.

# MyCompanyApp.Wpf - Product Vision

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:43

--------------------------------------------------
GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fully Offline Desktop Application
- No internet dependency after deployment
- Enterprise-grade stability
- Zero installation errors policy

--------------------------------------------------
USER INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Modern attractive UI
- Smooth animations
- Custom designed message windows
- Replace default MessageBox with custom dialog system
- Theme switching (Light / Dark / Custom Themes)
- Font switching capability
- Centralized UI styling engine
- Consistent visual identity

--------------------------------------------------
PERSIAN CALENDAR SUPPORT
--------------------------------------------------

- Full Jalali (Shamsi) date support
- Persian date picker
- Internal conversion utilities (Miladi <-> Shamsi)
- Database storage in standard format
- UI display in Persian format

--------------------------------------------------
REMINDER SYSTEM (Similar to Badsaba)
--------------------------------------------------

- Time-based reminders
- Background scheduler
- Notification popup system
- Snooze capability
- Recurring reminders
- Local storage (offline)

--------------------------------------------------
LOGGING SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Application logging
- Database logging
- Error logging
- Performance logging
- Log rotation
- Structured logging format
- Logs stored locally
- Optional database log storage

--------------------------------------------------
BACKUP SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Manual backup
- Automatic scheduled backup
- Database backup
- Restore mechanism
- Backup compression
- Backup versioning

--------------------------------------------------
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fast startup
- Optimized database queries
- Index optimization
- Async operations where required
- Memory usage control
- No UI freezing

--------------------------------------------------
DATABASE OPTIMIZATION
--------------------------------------------------

- Index strategy
- Query performance review
- Profiling support
- Maintenance routines

--------------------------------------------------
TESTABILITY
--------------------------------------------------

- Application layer testable
- Unit tests for core services
- Separated business logic
- No UI-dependent business logic

--------------------------------------------------
INSTALLATION REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Stable installer
- No dependency conflicts
- Clean publish output
- Ready-to-deploy package
- No runtime errors at customer side

--------------------------------------------------
USER MANAGEMENT & ACCESS CONTROL
--------------------------------------------------

- Role-based access control (RBAC)
- User management panel
- Create/Edit/Delete users
- Role definition system
- Permission-based section access
- Feature-level authorization
- Secure authentication
- Password hashing

--------------------------------------------------
FINAL PRODUCT GOAL
--------------------------------------------------

A fully offline, high-performance,
enterprise-grade Persian desktop system
with modern UI and zero instability.

# PROJECT EXECUTION ROADMAP
Generated: 06/15/2026 20:06:42

========================================
PROJECT CORE ARCHITECTURE
========================================

Architecture: Modular Clean Monolith
UI Framework: WPF + MVVM
Database: SQLite
ORM: Entity Framework Core
Security Model: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: EXE
Backup Strategy: Daily Backup (Max 3 Copies)

========================================
IDENTITY & SECURITY RULES
========================================

User Creation:
Only ADMIN can create users.

Authentication:
Username + Password

Password Reset:
- Admin can reset user passwords
- Users can reset their own password

Account Lockout Policy:
- Lock after 3 failed login attempts
- Automatic unlock after 30 minutes
- Admin can manually unlock accounts

Role Model:
- Each user has ONLY ONE role
- Roles are predefined but editable by Admin
- Admin can create / edit / delete roles

Permissions:
- Granular Permission System
- Stored in database
- Assigned to roles

========================================
CORE SYSTEM MODULES
========================================

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Dashboard
Reports
Backup
ImportExport
Settings
Logs
Notifications

All modules are REQUIRED.

========================================
CORE DOMAIN ENTITIES
========================================

Employee
Department
Task
Project
LeaveRequest
Attendance
Notification
ReportDefinition
AuditLog
Setting
BackupRecord
FileAttachment
CalendarEvent
ImportJob
ExportJob

DEVELOPMENT PHASES

==================================================

# PROJECT MASTER ROADMAP

This file is the Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Architecture Style: Modular Clean Monolith
Database: SQLite (WAL Enabled)
Security: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: Inno Setup

---

## 17. Implementation Strategy (Execution Freeze)

### 17.1 Implementation Priority (Sequencing)
1. **Database** (Infrastructure/Entities/Persistence)
2. **Security/Auth** (Login/RBAC/Lockout)
3. **Shell/Navigation** (UI Framework/Theme/Navigation)
4. **Dashboard**

### 17.2 Database & Encryption
- Start with stable SQLite.
- After stable migrations, implement SQLCipher.

### 17.3 Startup Flow
1. Splash Screen
2. Backup Check (Automatic)
3. Login
4. Dashboard

### 17.4 Administrative Flow
- First Run: Setup Wizard
- Admin password defined by user

### 17.5 Backup & Restore
- Automatic: Splash + On Exit
- Manual: Admin only
- Restore requires full restart

### 17.6 Module Development Order
1. Settings
2. Departments
3. Employees

### 17.7 Tech Stack Freeze
- UI: WPF + MaterialDesignInXaml
- Validation: FluentValidation
- Mapping: Mapster
- Repository: Aggregates only
- Audit Logs: CRUD + Security events
- Error Handling: Dialog (Critical) + Toast (Info)


==================================================

### 17.8 SSOT Clarification Decisions

These decisions clarify architecture and implementation rules and become binding SSOT rules.

---

DECISION 012 - First Run Bootstrap

On first launch, if no users exist, the application MUST start the Setup Wizard.
The Setup Wizard creates the first ADMIN account.
Username and password are defined by the installer user.
After the first ADMIN exists, the wizard MUST NOT run again.

---

DECISION 013 - Security and RBAC Clarifications

The system initializes with four base roles:

ADMIN
MANAGER
OPERATOR
VIEWER

ADMIN has full system access.
MANAGER has operational control over business modules.
OPERATOR can perform daily operational tasks.
VIEWER has read-only access.

Roles and permissions are stored in the database and editable by ADMIN.

---

DECISION 014 - Dynamic Field Model

Entities supporting dynamic fields in V1:

Customer
Employee
Document
OrganizationProfile

Dynamic fields are stored in metadata tables and rendered dynamically in UI forms.

Each dynamic field supports:

DataType
ValidationRules
IsRequired
IsVisible
DisplayOrder

---

DECISION 015 - Dynamic Reporting Runtime

Reports are metadata-driven.

ReportDefinition defines:

Entity source
Columns
Filters
Sort rules

Execution pipeline:

Metadata -> Query Builder -> EF Core Expression -> Result Grid

Raw SQL execution is not allowed in V1 reporting runtime.

---

DECISION 016 - Extensibility Implementation Rules

The architecture is modular but NOT plugin-based in V1.

Modules are compiled into the main application.

Future plugin architecture may support:

External module loading
Versioned extensions
Module registration

Plugin architecture is deferred until post-V1.

---

DECISION 017 - Backup and Restore Policy

Automatic backup triggers:

Application startup
Application exit

Manual backup:

ADMIN only

Backup includes:

SQLite database
Attachments folder
Critical configuration files

Restore requires full application restart.

Maximum backup copies retained: 3

---

DECISION 018 - Mandatory Modules for V1

The following modules are REQUIRED for version 1:

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Settings
Employees
Departments
Reports
Backup Manager
Logs
Dashboard

The application cannot be considered V1-complete without these modules.

---

DECISION 019 - Deferred Items After V1

The following features are postponed after initial release:

Plugin framework
Multi-organization support
Distributed sync
Advanced analytics engine
Microservice split

---

DECISION 020 - SSOT Precedence Rule

If documentation conflicts exist, priority order is:

1. PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md
2. PROJECT_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
3. PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md
4. PROJECT_IMPLEMENTATION_AUDIT.md

PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md is the final authority.

---

## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Decision Log

Decision-001:
Security system will be implemented ALL FEATURES but PHASED.

Phases:
1. Login + Password Hashing
2. Role Management
3. Permission System (RBAC)
4. Account Lockout
5. Admin Password Reset

Decision-002:
Documentation must be updated after every architectural clarification.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Architecture Decisions

Decision-003:
Roles are ADMIN DEFINED.
Only administrators can create, edit or delete roles.

Decision-004:
Each user can have ONLY ONE role.

Database relation:
User -> RoleId

Decision-005:
Permission model is GRANULAR.

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete

Task.Create
Task.Assign
Task.Complete

Permissions are assigned to Roles.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Decisions Continued

Decision-006:
Users have NO default role.
Admin must select a role during user creation.

Decision-007:
System does NOT include a SuperAdmin role.

Decision-008:
Permissions are stored in the DATABASE.

Decision-009:
Account lockout occurs after 5 failed login attempts.

Unlock policy:
Admin unlock only.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Settings System Decisions

Decision-010:
Settings have two levels.

Global Settings
User Settings

Decision-011:
Settings are stored in DATABASE.

Decision-012:
Settings are categorized.

Categories examples:
General
UI
Security
Backup
System

Decision-013:
Application includes Restore Default Settings feature.

Decision-014:
Every setting must include a DefaultValue.

## Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

`	ext

## Security Implementation Strategy

Security will be implemented using a phased RBAC model.

Core Components:
- User
- Role
- Permission

Security Features:
- Internal Login
- Password Hashing
- Role-Based Access Control
- Account Lockout
- Admin Password Reset


## RBAC Security Model

The application uses Role-Based Access Control.

Model:

User
  -> Role

Role
  -> Permissions

Permission naming convention:

Entity.Action

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete
Task.Assign
Report.View

Constraints:

- Each user has exactly ONE role
- Roles are created and managed by Admin
- Permissions are attached to roles


## Account Lockout Policy

Failed login attempts allowed: 5

After 5 failed attempts:
User account becomes LOCKED.

Unlock strategy:
Admin manual unlock only.

No automatic unlock.


## Settings System Architecture

Levels:

Global Settings
User Settings

Storage:
Database

Each setting contains:

Key
Value
DefaultValue
Category
Scope
Description

Hierarchy:

Default
 -> Global
 -> User

Users may override global settings.

Restore Default feature resets values to DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md

`	ext
# Final Architecture Audit Decisions

## Excel Import/Export
The system shall support Excel import and export. Excel import shall use a Smart Merge strategy with preview and validation before committing data. The system shall report new, updated, duplicated, and invalid records before final import.

## Password Policy
Passwords shall have a medium-strength policy suitable for an offline desktop system.

Rules:
Minimum length: 8 characters  
At least one letter  
At least one digit  
Special characters are optional  

## Installer
The production release shall use an EXE installer. The architecture shall remain compatible with a future MSI installer if enterprise deployment is required.

## Error Handling
The application shall show user-friendly Persian error messages and store full technical error details in both file logs and database logs.

## Backup Policy
The application shall create one daily backup during splash/startup initialization if no backup exists for the current Persian day.

Maximum backups kept: 3

Backup file names shall include the Persian date and time.

Example filename:

Backup_1405-03-25_14-30-10.db

## Logging

The system shall support both:

File logging  
Database logging  

Log categories:

Activity  
Security  
Error  
System  
Backup  
ImportExport  

## Dashboard

The dashboard shall display:

Quick statistics  
Live charts  
Notifications  
System health  
Backup status  

Recent activity shall NOT be displayed on the dashboard, although all activities shall still be logged.

## Notifications

The application shall support:

Toast notifications for immediate feedback  
Notification Center for persistent important notifications

## Modular Structure

The application architecture shall remain a Modular Clean Monolith.

Core modules:

Dashboard  
Identity  
Roles  
Permissions  
Settings  
Reports  
Logs  
Backup  
Notifications  
ImportExport  



``n---

## Source: DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

`	ext
# DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE

Project: MyCompanyApp.Wpf
Project Root: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

## Your Role

You are a senior software architect, WPF engineer, database designer, and AI-driven development assistant.

Your responsibility is to continue this project safely, professionally, and incrementally.

The user must only execute complete PowerShell scripts.
Never ask the user to manually edit project files.

## Mandatory First Step

Before answering or generating development code, you MUST ask the user to provide the latest contents or generated report from:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

If file contents are not available in the chat, generate a PowerShell script that reads these files and prints or exports their content for the user to send back.

## Daily Workflow

### Step 1 - Read Project Knowledge

Analyze the following documentation:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

### Step 2 - Analyze Current State

Detect and report:

- architectural weaknesses
- technical debt
- missing modules
- missing infrastructure
- unclear requirements
- database risks
- SQLite multi-user risks
- security risks
- build risks
- installer and deployment risks
- UI/UX inconsistencies
- documentation inconsistencies

### Step 3 - Ask Clarification Questions

Before continuing development, ask the user important clarification questions when needed.

Questions should focus on:

- database schema design
- entity relationships
- domain rules
- personnel workflows
- task workflows
- leave calculation edge cases
- Persian calendar behavior
- 30-day calendar calculation behavior
- reminder engine behavior
- notification rules
- advanced logging strategy
- backup safety
- role and permission rules
- report and export design
- installer reliability

Do not continue coding if critical design decisions are still unclear.

### Step 4 - Update Documentation Automatically

Whenever the user answers clarification questions, update these files automatically through a complete PowerShell script:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md

Never wait for the user to remind you to update documentation.

### Step 5 - Development Rules

All development output must be ONE complete PowerShell script.

The script must:

- create folders if needed
- create or update files
- create or update C# code
- update documentation when needed
- run dotnet restore
- run dotnet build
- stop on errors
- avoid partial snippets

The user should only copy and paste one PowerShell script.

### Step 6 - Architectural Rules

Maintain this architecture:

- Platform: reusable common infrastructure
- Modules: feature modules
- Apps: WPF application

Keep dependency direction clean.

UI must not contain business logic.
Domain must not depend on WPF.
Infrastructure must not leak into UI.

### Step 7 - Data Access Decision

Use Entity Framework Core with SQLite.

Rules:

- Use EF Core SQLite provider
- Hide DbContext behind repositories/application services where appropriate
- Use UnitOfWork pattern where useful
- Use EF Core Change Tracking or Interceptors for advanced audit logging
- Enable safe SQLite settings for multi-user usage:
  - WAL mode
  - busy timeout
  - short transactions
  - retry handling where possible
- Avoid long-running write transactions

### Step 8 - Product Goal

Build a stable offline multi-user WPF desktop platform in Persian with:

- personnel management
- editable departments
- editable personnel statuses
- authentication and authorization
- checkbox-based permission management
- task management with steps
- multi-user task assignment
- task dependencies
- leave management
- Persian calendar
- normal and 30-day calculation mode per leave/reminder record
- official holidays
- admin-defined holidays
- reminder engine
- snooze/repeat/windows notification settings per reminder
- advanced audit logging
- reports
- Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export
- backup and restore
- simple manual update model
- professional Windows 11 style UI
- friendly Persian messages

### Step 9 - Session Output Format

Every session response must include:

1. Project understanding summary
2. Detected weaknesses and risks
3. Clarification questions, if needed
4. Proposed next safe development step
5. One complete PowerShell script, only when enough information exists
6. Reminder of what file/report the user should send next time

Wait for user confirmation before moving to the next phase.


``n---

# Architecture Decisions ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ 2026-06-16

These decisions were confirmed during architecture clarification.

## Decision 001 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Database Naming Convention
Chosen Option: **d**

## Decision 002 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Domain Entity Strategy
Chosen Option: **b**

## Decision 003 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Audit Logging Strategy
Chosen Model: **Hybrid Audit**

Meaning:
- Critical actions logged in database
- Technical logs stored in file logs
- Security events duplicated for safety

## Decision 004 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Task Status Design
Implementation: **TaskStatus Table**

Reason:
Task status must be configurable and extensible instead of hard-coded enum.

## Decision 005 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reminder System
Architecture: **Generic Reminder Engine**

Capabilities:
- Works with multiple modules
- Supports snooze / repeat
- Supports multiple notification channels

## Decision 006 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Import Architecture
Strategy: **ImportService per module**

Example:
- EmployeeImportService
- DepartmentImportService
- TaskImportService

Advantages:
- Clear validation rules
- Module isolation
- Easier maintenance

## Decision 007 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reporting Filters
Design:
**Dynamic Filter + Predefined Dataset**

Meaning:
- User-defined filters
- Prebuilt datasets for common reports
- Flexible reporting layer

## Decision 008 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Backup Configuration
Backup location controlled by:

**BackupLocation Setting**

Stored in:
System Settings table.

## Decision 009 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Architecture Choice
Chosen Option: **c**

## Decision 010 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Final Architecture Opinion

Recommended approach:

Hybrid enterprise desktop architecture combining:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- Metadata-driven reporting
- Dynamic field configuration
- Generic reminder engine
- RBAC security model

This ensures:

- maintainability
- scalability
- modular growth
- enterprise stability

## Decision 011 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Selected Option
Chosen Option: **a**

# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## 1. Architectural Intent

The project supports an extensibility model centered on:

- metadata-driven reporting
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven settings
- controlled customization per organization
- maintainable core architecture

The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.

---

## 2. Confirmed Dynamic Components

The roadmap already includes the following extensibility-related building blocks:

- EntityFieldSetting
- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

These components are used to support:

- field activation and visibility
- optional fields
- database-driven report definitions
- configurable report columns
- configurable report filters
- configurable report sorting

---

## 3. Customization Rules

Any customization must follow these rules:

1. **Database-driven first**
   - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.

2. **Core stability**
   - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.

3. **Explicit field control**
   - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.

4. **Report configuration separation**
   - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.

5. **Safe optional-field strategy**
   - Optional entity fields should follow the existing EmployeeOptionalFields approach when a structured extension is needed.

---

## 4. Dynamic Field Strategy

The project should use dynamic fields for cases where:

- a field may be enabled or disabled per deployment
- a field is optional for some organizations
- a field requires visibility control
- a field needs metadata-based behavior

The roadmap already confirms metadata-driven field activation and visibility, so this is the official direction for dynamic field behavior.

---

## 5. Dynamic Reporting Strategy

The reporting layer should remain metadata-driven.

That means reports can be defined using:

- a report definition
- selected columns
- filters
- sorting rules

This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

---

## 6. Field Settings Service

The presence of:

- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService

confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.

This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

---

## 7. Extensibility Boundary

This extensibility model is **not** a plugin framework.

It is a controlled metadata-driven customization approach for:

- field behavior
- report behavior
- optional structured data
- settings-based runtime decisions

No plugin architecture is introduced by this document unless explicitly added later in the SSOT.

---

## 8. Resulting Design Direction

The confirmed design direction is:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- metadata-driven customization
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven reporting metadata
- maintainable and enterprise-stable implementation

This keeps the system flexible while protecting the core codebase.

---

# ENTERPRISE DATABASE BLUEPRINT

## 1. Purpose

This section defines the official enterprise-grade database blueprint for the project.

The database must support:

- Modular Clean Monolith architecture
- WPF desktop deployment
- SQLite as the local relational database engine
- EF Core as the ORM
- RBAC security model
- auditability
- soft delete
- dynamic field configuration
- metadata-driven reporting
- reminder engine
- backup and restore
- maintainable future growth

This blueprint is part of the Single Source of Truth and must be used as the reference for all future entity, DbContext, migration, repository, and service implementations.

---

## 2. Database Design Rules

### 2.1 Common Entity Columns

Most business tables should include the following fields:

| Column | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Id | INTEGER / GUID | Yes | Primary key |
| CreatedAt | DateTime | Yes | Creation timestamp |
| CreatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who created the record |
| UpdatedAt | DateTime | No | Last update timestamp |
| UpdatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who last updated the record |
| IsDeleted | Boolean | Yes | Soft delete flag |
| DeletedAt | DateTime | No | Delete timestamp |
| DeletedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who deleted the record |
| RowVersion | INTEGER / BLOB | No | Concurrency control |

### 2.2 Soft Delete Rule

Business records should not be physically deleted by default.

Instead:

- IsDeleted = true
- DeletedAt is set
- DeletedByUserId is set when available

Hard delete is only allowed for temporary, cache, or system maintenance tables.

### 2.3 Audit Rule

Important business actions must be auditable.

Audit logs should capture:

- who performed the action
- what entity was affected
- what operation was performed
- when it happened
- previous values where practical
- new values where practical

### 2.4 Metadata Rule

Dynamic fields, dynamic reports, and configurable behavior must be database-driven.

Confirmed metadata-driven concepts include:

- EntityFieldSetting
- dynamic field activation
- dynamic field visibility
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

### 2.5 SQLite Compatibility Rule

The schema must remain compatible with SQLite.

Avoid database features that are not portable or are difficult to maintain in SQLite, such as:

- stored procedures
- advanced server-side jobs
- database-specific procedural logic
- complex trigger-heavy behavior unless absolutely necessary

Business rules should primarily live in the application layer.

---

## 3. Table Groups Overview

The database is divided into the following logical groups:

1. Identity and Security
2. Organization and Application Settings
3. People and Business Entities
4. Documents and Attachments
5. Dynamic Fields and Metadata
6. Reporting
7. Reminder and Notification Engine
8. Audit and Activity Tracking
9. Backup and Maintenance
10. Import / Export
11. System Diagnostics

---

## 4. Identity and Security Tables

### 4.1 Users

Stores application users.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Username
- PasswordHash
- PasswordSalt
- DisplayName
- Email
- PhoneNumber
- IsActive
- IsSystemUser
- LastLoginAt
- FailedLoginCount
- LockoutEndAt
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId
- IsDeleted
- DeletedAt
- DeletedByUserId

### 4.2 Roles

Stores security roles.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Name
- NormalizedName
- Description
- IsSystemRole
- IsActive
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId
- IsDeleted

### 4.3 Permissions

Stores granular permissions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- PermissionKey
- ModuleKey
- DisplayName
- Description
- IsSystemPermission
- CreatedAt
- UpdatedAt

Examples:

- Customers.View
- Customers.Create
- Customers.Update
- Customers.Delete
- Reports.View
- Reports.Export
- Settings.Manage
- Backup.Create
- Backup.Restore

### 4.4 UserRoles

Many-to-many relationship between users and roles.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- RoleId
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId

### 4.5 RolePermissions

Many-to-many relationship between roles and permissions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- RoleId
- PermissionId
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId

### 4.6 UserPermissionOverrides

Optional direct allow/deny permission overrides for a user.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- PermissionId
- IsAllowed
- Reason
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId

### 4.7 UserSessions

Tracks login sessions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- LoginAt
- LogoutAt
- MachineName
- WindowsUsername
- AppVersion
- IpAddress
- IsActive

### 4.8 LoginHistory

Stores login attempts.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Username
- UserId

- FailureReason
- AttemptedAt
- MachineName
- WindowsUsername
- AppVersion

## 15. Recommended Initial Implementation Order

Database implementation should be done in this order:

1. Base entity abstractions
2. Users
3. Roles
4. Permissions
5. UserRoles
6. RolePermissions
7. SystemSettings
8. OrganizationProfile
9. LookupCategories
10. LookupItems
11. Customers
12. Employees
13. EmployeeOptionalFields
14. EntityFieldSettings
15. ReportDefinitions
16. ReportColumns
17. ReportFilters
18. ReportSorts
19. AuditLogs
20. ErrorLogs
21. BackupSettings
22. BackupJobs
23. Reminders
24. Notifications

This sequence allows the system to bootstrap security, settings, domain data, metadata, reporting, logging, and backup gradually.

## 17. Implementation Notes

This blueprint is intentionally broader than the first development phase.

Not all tables must be implemented immediately.

The first implementation milestone should focus on:

- identity and security
- settings
- customers
- employees
- dynamic fields
- dynamic reports
- audit logs
- error logs
- backup settings

Additional tables should be implemented when their module becomes active.
