# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## Platform Strategy

MyCompanyApp is designed as an **Extensible Modular Platform**.

The core system provides a stable foundation while allowing organizations to extend the system safely.

Target use cases:

- Internal organizational management
- Custom deployments for different organizations
- Future vertical solutions built on the same platform

Each installation is **singleط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â‚¬ع©organization**, but highly customizable.

---

## Customization Goals

The system must allow future extension without modifying core architecture.

Supported extensions:

- Add new tables
- Add new forms
- Add new modules
- Add new reports
- Add new fields to existing entities

---

## Metadata Driven Extension

The platform supports metadata-driven configuration.

Key components:

EntityFieldSetting  
ReportDefinition  
ReportColumn  
ReportFilter  
ReportSort  

These allow:

- dynamic fields
- dynamic reports
- configurable UI elements

---

## Dynamic Field System

Entities may support **optional fields** stored separately.

Example:

EmployeeOptionalFields

This pattern allows organizations to add fields without altering core schema.

---

## Module Extension Model

New modules can be added following the modular architecture.

Each module may include:

Domain Entities  
Application Services  
Views / ViewModels  
Import/Export Services  
Reports

Modules remain isolated from the core platform.

---

## Database Extension Strategy

When organizations require additional data structures, developers may add new tables that integrate with existing entities via foreign keys.

Example:

EmployeeTrainingRecords  
DepartmentBudgets  
ProjectContracts

These extensions must follow the same architectural rules.

---

## UI Extension Strategy

New forms can be added by creating new WPF Views and ViewModels that integrate into the navigation system.

Navigation is service-driven and supports dynamic module registration.

---

## Long Term Goal

Transform the system into a **stable enterprise desktop platform** that supports:

- organizational customization
- modular expansion
- metadata-driven configuration
- maintainable long-term architecture

---

---

# 18. EXTENSIBILITY / CUSTOMIZATION ARCHITECTURE

## 18.1 Architectural Goal

The system is designed as an **Extensible Modular Platform** for enterprise desktop deployments.

The core application must remain stable, while each organization can receive its own customized version of the system with:
- additional forms
- additional tables
- additional fields
- additional reports
- organization-specific workflows
- module-specific logic

This approach allows the product to be reused across different clients without rewriting the core application.

---

## 18.2 Deployment Model

The application is **single-organization per installation**.

This means:
- one installation belongs to one client / organization
- the system is not a shared multi-tenant SaaS platform
- customization is applied per deployment
- each client can have its own schema extensions and UI extensions

This model is intentionally chosen for:
- desktop enterprise usage
- on-premise installations
- customer-specific workflows
- controlled support and backup policies

---

## 18.3 Supported Extension Types

The platform must support the following types of extension:

### Database extensions
- new tables
- new columns where appropriate
- new foreign-key relationships
- new lookup tables
- new domain-specific entities

### UI extensions
- new WPF windows
- new WPF pages/views
- new view models
- new dialogs
- new navigation entries

### Metadata extensions
- dynamic fields
- field visibility rules
- validation rules
- report definitions
- filters and sorting rules

### Module extensions
- new functional modules
- isolated services
- module-specific import/export logic
- module-specific audit integration

---

## 18.4 Customization Principles

All customization must follow these rules:

1. **Protect the core architecture**
   - Core modules must stay clean and stable.
   - Custom behavior should extend the platform, not damage it.

2. **Prefer metadata over hardcoding**
   - Use configuration tables and definitions whenever possible.

3. **Keep custom features modular**
   - Each business area should remain isolated.
   - Do not mix unrelated custom logic into shared infrastructure.

4. **Preserve upgradeability**
   - Future updates must remain possible without destroying custom client data.

5. **Use explicit extension points**
   - Custom modules, forms, and tables must have clear registration and ownership.

---

## 18.5 Metadata-Driven Design

To support flexible organization-specific behavior, the system will use metadata-driven structures where practical.

Possible metadata entities include:
- EntityFieldDefinition
- EntityFieldValue
- NavigationItemDefinition
- FormDefinition
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumnDefinition
- ReportFilterDefinition
- ValidationRuleDefinition

These structures will support:
- dynamic UI fields
- configurable navigation
- dynamic reporting
- validation without core rewrites
- client-specific presentation rules

---

## 18.6 Dynamic Field Strategy

When an organization needs extra fields for an existing entity, the preferred strategy is:

### Option A: Metadata-backed fields
Use field definitions and value tables to store variable data.

Best for:
- optional fields
- organization-specific properties
- configurable UI scenarios

### Option B: Extension tables
Create a dedicated table linked to the main entity.

Best for:
- structured business data
- long-term stable extensions
- performance-sensitive data

### Design rule
Use metadata for flexibility, and extension tables for durable business requirements.

---

## 18.7 Dynamic Form Strategy

The UI must support adding new forms without modifying the shell architecture.

Each module should be able to register:
- its views
- its view models
- its commands
- its navigation items
- its validation rules

Recommended pattern:
- module-level ViewModel registration via DI
- shell-level navigation only
- consistent MaterialDesign styling across modules
- isolated form logic per module

---

## 18.8 Dynamic Report Strategy

Reports should support both static and configurable definitions.

The report system should allow:
- adding new report definitions
- adding report columns
- adding report filters
- changing grouping and sorting rules
- exporting to common formats

This will let client-specific reporting needs be implemented without rewriting the reporting engine.

---

## 18.9 Module Registration Strategy

Future modules must be designed to plug into the application in a controlled way.

Each module should expose:
- domain entities
- application services
- views
- view models
- validation
- mapping profiles
- import/export support
- audit hooks if needed

The main shell should only handle:
- module discovery
- module registration
- navigation integration
- service composition

---

## 18.10 Database Growth Strategy

The database design must support controlled expansion.

Rules:
- core tables remain normalized
- extension tables are linked with foreign keys
- audit fields are preserved
- soft delete is used where appropriate
- schema changes must remain explicit and maintainable

When a client requires new business data:
- prefer a dedicated table
- link it to the owning entity
- avoid overusing generic columns for unrelated data

---

## 18.11 Long-Term Outcome

The project is not only a fixed internal application.

It is intended to become a **stable enterprise desktop platform** that can be adapted for different organizations while preserving:
- maintainability
- security
- clarity
- upgradeability
- modular growth

This extensibility direction is now a formal architectural decision.

---

# PROJECT EXECUTION ROADMAP
Generated: 06/15/2026 20:06:42

========================================
PROJECT CORE ARCHITECTURE
========================================

Architecture: Modular Clean Monolith
UI Framework: WPF + MVVM
Database: SQLite
ORM: Entity Framework Core
Security Model: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: EXE
Backup Strategy: Daily Backup (Max 3 Copies)

========================================
IDENTITY & SECURITY RULES
========================================

User Creation:
Only ADMIN can create users.

Authentication:
Username + Password

Password Reset:
- Admin can reset user passwords
- Users can reset their own password

Account Lockout Policy:
- Lock after 3 failed login attempts
- Automatic unlock after 30 minutes
- Admin can manually unlock accounts

Role Model:
- Each user has ONLY ONE role
- Roles are predefined but editable by Admin
- Admin can create / edit / delete roles

Permissions:
- Granular Permission System
- Stored in database
- Assigned to roles

========================================
CORE SYSTEM MODULES
========================================

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Dashboard
Reports
Backup
ImportExport
Settings
Logs
Notifications

All modules are REQUIRED.

========================================
CORE DOMAIN ENTITIES
========================================

Employee
Department
Task
Project
LeaveRequest
Attendance
Notification
ReportDefinition
AuditLog
Setting
BackupRecord
FileAttachment
CalendarEvent
ImportJob
ExportJob

========================================
DEVELOPMENT PHASES
========================================

==================================================

# PROJECT MASTER ROADMAP

This file is the Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Architecture Style: Modular Clean Monolith
Database: SQLite (WAL Enabled)
Security: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: Inno Setup

---

## 17. Implementation Strategy (Execution Freeze)

### 17.1 Implementation Priority (Sequencing)
1. **Database** (Infrastructure/Entities/Persistence)
2. **Security/Auth** (Login/RBAC/Lockout)
3. **Shell/Navigation** (UI Framework/Theme/Navigation)
4. **Dashboard**

### 17.2 Database & Encryption
- Start with stable SQLite.
- After stable migrations, implement SQLCipher.

### 17.3 Startup Flow
1. Splash Screen
2. Backup Check (Automatic)
3. Login
4. Dashboard

### 17.4 Administrative Flow
- First Run: Setup Wizard
- Admin password defined by user

### 17.5 Backup & Restore
- Automatic: Splash + On Exit
- Manual: Admin only
- Restore requires full restart

### 17.6 Module Development Order
1. Settings
2. Departments
3. Employees

### 17.7 Tech Stack Freeze
- UI: WPF + MaterialDesignInXaml
- Validation: FluentValidation
- Mapping: Mapster
- Repository: Aggregates only
- Audit Logs: CRUD + Security events
- Error Handling: Dialog (Critical) + Toast (Info)

- ask clarification questions when needed
- update documentation automatically

- run restore and build inside scripts
- preserve clean architecture
- prioritize stability and maintainability

## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Decision Log

Decision-001:
Security system will be implemented ALL FEATURES but PHASED.

Phases:
1. Login + Password Hashing
2. Role Management
3. Permission System (RBAC)
4. Account Lockout
5. Admin Password Reset

Decision-002:
Documentation must be updated after every architectural clarification.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Architecture Decisions

Decision-003:
Roles are ADMIN DEFINED.
Only administrators can create, edit or delete roles.

Decision-004:
Each user can have ONLY ONE role.

Database relation:
User -> RoleId

Decision-005:
Permission model is GRANULAR.

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete

Task.Create
Task.Assign
Task.Complete

Permissions are assigned to Roles.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Decisions Continued

Decision-006:
Users have NO default role.
Admin must select a role during user creation.

Decision-007:
System does NOT include a SuperAdmin role.

Decision-008:
Permissions are stored in the DATABASE.

Decision-009:
Account lockout occurs after 5 failed login attempts.

Unlock policy:
Admin unlock only.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Settings System Decisions

Decision-010:
Settings have two levels.

Global Settings
User Settings

Decision-011:
Settings are stored in DATABASE.

Decision-012:
Settings are categorized.

Categories examples:
General
UI
Security
Backup
System

Decision-013:
Application includes Restore Default Settings feature.

Decision-014:
Every setting must include a DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

`	ext

## Security Implementation Strategy

Security will be implemented using a phased RBAC model.

Core Components:
- User
- Role
- Permission

Security Features:
- Internal Login
- Password Hashing
- Role-Based Access Control
- Account Lockout
- Admin Password Reset


## RBAC Security Model

The application uses Role-Based Access Control.

Model:

User
  -> Role

Role
  -> Permissions

Permission naming convention:

Entity.Action

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete
Task.Assign
Report.View

Constraints:

- Each user has exactly ONE role
- Roles are created and managed by Admin
- Permissions are attached to roles


## Account Lockout Policy

Failed login attempts allowed: 5

After 5 failed attempts:
User account becomes LOCKED.

Unlock strategy:
Admin manual unlock only.

No automatic unlock.


## Settings System Architecture

Levels:

Global Settings
User Settings

Storage:
Database

Each setting contains:

Key
Value
DefaultValue
Category
Scope
Description

Hierarchy:

Default
 -> Global
 -> User

Users may override global settings.

Restore Default feature resets values to DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md

`	ext
# Final Architecture Audit Decisions

## Excel Import/Export
The system shall support Excel import and export. Excel import shall use a Smart Merge strategy with preview and validation before committing data. The system shall report new, updated, duplicated, and invalid records before final import.

## Password Policy
Passwords shall have a medium-strength policy suitable for an offline desktop system.

Rules:
Minimum length: 8 characters  
At least one letter  
At least one digit  
Special characters are optional  

## Installer
The production release shall use an EXE installer. The architecture shall remain compatible with a future MSI installer if enterprise deployment is required.

## Error Handling
The application shall show user-friendly Persian error messages and store full technical error details in both file logs and database logs.

## Backup Policy
The application shall create one daily backup during splash/startup initialization if no backup exists for the current Persian day.

Maximum backups kept: 3

Backup file names shall include the Persian date and time.

Example filename:

Backup_1405-03-25_14-30-10.db

## Logging

The system shall support both:

File logging  
Database logging  

Log categories:

Activity  
Security  
Error  
System  
Backup  
ImportExport  

## Dashboard

The dashboard shall display:

Quick statistics  
Live charts  
Notifications  
System health  
Backup status  

Recent activity shall NOT be displayed on the dashboard, although all activities shall still be logged.

## Notifications

The application shall support:

Toast notifications for immediate feedback  
Notification Center for persistent important notifications

## Modular Structure

The application architecture shall remain a Modular Clean Monolith.

Core modules:

Dashboard  
Identity  
Roles  
Permissions  
Settings  
Reports  
Logs  
Backup  
Notifications  
ImportExport  



``n---

## Source: DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

`	ext
# DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE

Project: MyCompanyApp.Wpf
Project Root: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

## Your Role

You are a senior software architect, WPF engineer, database designer, and AI-driven development assistant.

Your responsibility is to continue this project safely, professionally, and incrementally.

The user must only execute complete PowerShell scripts.
Never ask the user to manually edit project files.

## Mandatory First Step

Before answering or generating development code, you MUST ask the user to provide the latest contents or generated report from:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

If file contents are not available in the chat, generate a PowerShell script that reads these files and prints or exports their content for the user to send back.

## Daily Workflow

### Step 1 - Read Project Knowledge

Analyze the following documentation:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

### Step 2 - Analyze Current State

Detect and report:

- architectural weaknesses
- technical debt
- missing modules
- missing infrastructure
- unclear requirements
- database risks
- SQLite multi-user risks
- security risks
- build risks
- installer and deployment risks
- UI/UX inconsistencies
- documentation inconsistencies

### Step 3 - Ask Clarification Questions

Before continuing development, ask the user important clarification questions when needed.

Questions should focus on:

- database schema design
- entity relationships
- domain rules
- personnel workflows
- task workflows
- leave calculation edge cases
- Persian calendar behavior
- 30-day calendar calculation behavior
- reminder engine behavior
- notification rules
- advanced logging strategy
- backup safety
- role and permission rules
- report and export design
- installer reliability

Do not continue coding if critical design decisions are still unclear.

### Step 4 - Update Documentation Automatically

Whenever the user answers clarification questions, update these files automatically through a complete PowerShell script:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md

Never wait for the user to remind you to update documentation.

### Step 5 - Development Rules

All development output must be ONE complete PowerShell script.

The script must:

- create folders if needed
- create or update files
- create or update C# code
- update documentation when needed
- run dotnet restore
- run dotnet build
- stop on errors
- avoid partial snippets

The user should only copy and paste one PowerShell script.

### Step 6 - Architectural Rules

Maintain this architecture:

- Platform: reusable common infrastructure
- Modules: feature modules
- Apps: WPF application

Keep dependency direction clean.

UI must not contain business logic.
Domain must not depend on WPF.
Infrastructure must not leak into UI.

### Step 7 - Data Access Decision

Use Entity Framework Core with SQLite.

Rules:

- Use EF Core SQLite provider
- Hide DbContext behind repositories/application services where appropriate
- Use UnitOfWork pattern where useful
- Use EF Core Change Tracking or Interceptors for advanced audit logging
- Enable safe SQLite settings for multi-user usage:
  - WAL mode
  - busy timeout
  - short transactions
  - retry handling where possible
- Avoid long-running write transactions

### Step 8 - Product Goal

Build a stable offline multi-user WPF desktop platform in Persian with:

- personnel management
- editable departments
- editable personnel statuses
- authentication and authorization
- checkbox-based permission management
- task management with steps
- multi-user task assignment
- task dependencies
- leave management
- Persian calendar
- normal and 30-day calculation mode per leave/reminder record
- official holidays
- admin-defined holidays
- reminder engine
- snooze/repeat/windows notification settings per reminder
- advanced audit logging
- reports
- Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export
- backup and restore
- simple manual update model
- professional Windows 11 style UI
- friendly Persian messages

### Step 9 - Session Output Format

Every session response must include:

1. Project understanding summary
2. Detected weaknesses and risks
3. Clarification questions, if needed
4. Proposed next safe development step
5. One complete PowerShell script, only when enough information exists
6. Reminder of what file/report the user should send next time

Wait for user confirmation before moving to the next phase.


``n---

- Added EntityFieldSetting for metadata-driven field activation and visibility.
- Added ReportDefinition, ReportColumn, ReportFilter, and ReportSort as the base model for dynamic reports.
- Added EmployeeOptionalFields as a safe optional-field pattern for future entity expansion.
- Introduced initial IEntityFieldSettingsService / EntityFieldSettingsService application service.
- The implementation follows the documented rule that settings and report metadata must remain database-driven and documentation must be updated automatically.

# Architecture Decisions ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ 2026-06-16

These decisions were confirmed during architecture clarification.

## Decision 001 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Database Naming Convention
Chosen Option: **d**

## Decision 002 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Domain Entity Strategy
Chosen Option: **b**

## Decision 003 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Audit Logging Strategy
Chosen Model: **Hybrid Audit**

Meaning:
- Critical actions logged in database
- Technical logs stored in file logs
- Security events duplicated for safety

## Decision 004 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Task Status Design
Implementation: **TaskStatus Table**

Reason:
Task status must be configurable and extensible instead of hard-coded enum.

## Decision 005 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reminder System
Architecture: **Generic Reminder Engine**

Capabilities:
- Works with multiple modules
- Supports snooze / repeat
- Supports multiple notification channels

## Decision 006 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Import Architecture
Strategy: **ImportService per module**

Example:
- EmployeeImportService
- DepartmentImportService
- TaskImportService

Advantages:
- Clear validation rules
- Module isolation
- Easier maintenance

## Decision 007 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reporting Filters
Design:
**Dynamic Filter + Predefined Dataset**

Meaning:
- User-defined filters
- Prebuilt datasets for common reports
- Flexible reporting layer

## Decision 008 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Backup Configuration

## Decision 010 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Final Architecture Opinion

Recommended approach:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- Metadata-driven reporting
- Dynamic field configuration
- Generic reminder engine
- RBAC security model

This ensures:

- maintainability
- scalability

## Decision 011 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Selected Option

# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## 1. Architectural Intent

The project supports an extensibility model centered on:

- metadata-driven reporting
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven settings
- controlled customization per organization
- maintainable core architecture

The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.

---

## 2. Confirmed Dynamic Components

The roadmap already includes the following extensibility-related building blocks:

- EntityFieldSetting
- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

These components are used to support:

- field activation and visibility
- optional fields
- database-driven report definitions
- configurable report columns
- configurable report filters
- configurable report sorting

---

## 3. Customization Rules

Any customization must follow these rules:

1. **Database-driven first**
   - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.

2. **Core stability**
   - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.

3. **Explicit field control**
   - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.

4. **Report configuration separation**
   - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.

5. **Safe optional-field strategy**
   - Optional entity fields should follow the existing EmployeeOptionalFields approach when a structured extension is needed.

---

## 4. Dynamic Field Strategy

The project should use dynamic fields for cases where:

- a field may be enabled or disabled per deployment
- a field is optional for some organizations
- a field requires visibility control
- a field needs metadata-based behavior

The roadmap already confirms metadata-driven field activation and visibility, so this is the official direction for dynamic field behavior.

---

## 5. Dynamic Reporting Strategy

The reporting layer should remain metadata-driven.

That means reports can be defined using:

- a report definition
- selected columns
- filters
- sorting rules

This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

---

## 6. Field Settings Service

The presence of:

- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService

confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.

This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

---

## 7. Extensibility Boundary

This extensibility model is **not** a plugin framework.

It is a controlled metadata-driven customization approach for:

- field behavior
- report behavior
- optional structured data
- settings-based runtime decisions

No plugin architecture is introduced by this document unless explicitly added later in the SSOT.

---

## 8. Resulting Design Direction

The confirmed design direction is:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- metadata-driven customization
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven reporting metadata
- maintainable and enterprise-stable implementation

This keeps the system flexible while protecting the core codebase.

--------------------------------------

# PROJECT EXECUTION ROADMAP
Generated: 06/15/2026 20:06:42

========================================
PROJECT CORE ARCHITECTURE
========================================

Architecture: Modular Clean Monolith
UI Framework: WPF + MVVM
Database: SQLite
ORM: Entity Framework Core
Security Model: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: EXE
Backup Strategy: Daily Backup (Max 3 Copies)

========================================
IDENTITY & SECURITY RULES
========================================

User Creation:
Only ADMIN can create users.

Authentication:
Username + Password

Password Reset:
- Admin can reset user passwords
- Users can reset their own password

Account Lockout Policy:
- Lock after 3 failed login attempts
- Automatic unlock after 30 minutes
- Admin can manually unlock accounts

Role Model:
- Each user has ONLY ONE role
- Roles are predefined but editable by Admin
- Admin can create / edit / delete roles

Permissions:
- Granular Permission System
- Stored in database
- Assigned to roles

========================================
CORE SYSTEM MODULES
========================================

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Dashboard
Reports
Backup
ImportExport
Settings
Logs
Notifications

All modules are REQUIRED.

========================================
CORE DOMAIN ENTITIES
========================================

Employee
Department
Task
Project
LeaveRequest
Attendance
Notification
ReportDefinition
AuditLog
Setting
BackupRecord
FileAttachment
CalendarEvent
ImportJob
ExportJob

========================================
DEVELOPMENT PHASES
========================================

==================================================

# PROJECT MASTER ROADMAP

This file is the Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Architecture Style: Modular Clean Monolith
Database: SQLite (WAL Enabled)
Security: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: Inno Setup

---

## 17. Implementation Strategy (Execution Freeze)

### 17.1 Implementation Priority (Sequencing)
1. **Database** (Infrastructure/Entities/Persistence)
2. **Security/Auth** (Login/RBAC/Lockout)
3. **Shell/Navigation** (UI Framework/Theme/Navigation)
4. **Dashboard**

### 17.2 Database & Encryption
- Start with stable SQLite.
- After stable migrations, implement SQLCipher.

### 17.3 Startup Flow
1. Splash Screen
2. Backup Check (Automatic)
3. Login
4. Dashboard

### 17.4 Administrative Flow
- First Run: Setup Wizard
- Admin password defined by user

### 17.5 Backup & Restore
- Automatic: Splash + On Exit
- Manual: Admin only
- Restore requires full restart

### 17.6 Module Development Order
1. Settings
2. Departments
3. Employees

### 17.7 Tech Stack Freeze
- UI: WPF + MaterialDesignInXaml
- Validation: FluentValidation
- Mapping: Mapster
- Repository: Aggregates only
- Audit Logs: CRUD + Security events
- Error Handling: Dialog (Critical) + Toast (Info)


==================================================

- ask clarification questions when needed
- update documentation automatically

- run restore and build inside scripts
- preserve clean architecture
- prioritize stability and maintainability

## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Decision Log

Decision-001:
Security system will be implemented ALL FEATURES but PHASED.

Phases:
1. Login + Password Hashing
2. Role Management
3. Permission System (RBAC)
4. Account Lockout
5. Admin Password Reset

Decision-002:
Documentation must be updated after every architectural clarification.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Architecture Decisions

Decision-003:
Roles are ADMIN DEFINED.
Only administrators can create, edit or delete roles.

Decision-004:
Each user can have ONLY ONE role.

Database relation:
User -> RoleId

Decision-005:
Permission model is GRANULAR.

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete

Task.Create
Task.Assign
Task.Complete

Permissions are assigned to Roles.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Decisions Continued

Decision-006:
Users have NO default role.
Admin must select a role during user creation.

Decision-007:
System does NOT include a SuperAdmin role.

Decision-008:
Permissions are stored in the DATABASE.

Decision-009:
Account lockout occurs after 5 failed login attempts.

Unlock policy:
Admin unlock only.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Settings System Decisions

Global Settings
User Settings

Settings are stored in DATABASE.

General
UI
Security
Backup
System

## Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

`	ext

## Security Implementation Strategy

Security will be implemented using a phased RBAC model.

Core Components:
- User
- Role
- Permission

Security Features:
- Internal Login
- Password Hashing
- Role-Based Access Control
- Account Lockout
- Admin Password Reset


## RBAC Security Model

The application uses Role-Based Access Control.

Model:

User
  -> Role

Role
  -> Permissions

Permission naming convention:

Entity.Action

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete
Task.Assign
Report.View

Constraints:

- Each user has exactly ONE role
- Roles are created and managed by Admin
- Permissions are attached to roles


## Account Lockout Policy

Failed login attempts allowed: 5

After 5 failed attempts:
User account becomes LOCKED.

Unlock strategy:
Admin manual unlock only.

No automatic unlock.


## Settings System Architecture

Levels:

Global Settings
User Settings

Storage:
Database

Each setting contains:

Key
Value
DefaultValue
Category
Scope
Description

Hierarchy:

Default
 -> Global
 -> User

Users may override global settings.

Restore Default feature resets values to DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md

`	ext
# Final Architecture Audit Decisions

## Excel Import/Export
The system shall support Excel import and export. Excel import shall use a Smart Merge strategy with preview and validation before committing data. The system shall report new, updated, duplicated, and invalid records before final import.

## Password Policy
Passwords shall have a medium-strength policy suitable for an offline desktop system.

Rules:
Minimum length: 8 characters  
At least one letter  
At least one digit  
Special characters are optional  

## Installer
The production release shall use an EXE installer. The architecture shall remain compatible with a future MSI installer if enterprise deployment is required.

## Error Handling
The application shall show user-friendly Persian error messages and store full technical error details in both file logs and database logs.

## Backup Policy
The application shall create one daily backup during splash/startup initialization if no backup exists for the current Persian day.

Maximum backups kept: 3

Backup file names shall include the Persian date and time.

Example filename:

Backup_1405-03-25_14-30-10.db

## Logging

The system shall support both:

File logging  
Database logging  

Log categories:

Activity  
Security  
Error  
System  
Backup  
ImportExport  

## Dashboard

The dashboard shall display:

Quick statistics  
Live charts  
Notifications  
System health  
Backup status  

Recent activity shall NOT be displayed on the dashboard, although all activities shall still be logged.

## Notifications

The application shall support:

Toast notifications for immediate feedback  
Notification Center for persistent important notifications

## Modular Structure

The application architecture shall remain a Modular Clean Monolith.

Core modules:

Dashboard  
Identity  
Roles  
Permissions  
Settings  
Reports  
Logs  
Backup  
Notifications  
ImportExport  



``n---

## Source: DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

`	ext
# DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE

Project: MyCompanyApp.Wpf
Project Root: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

## Your Role

You are a senior software architect, WPF engineer, database designer, and AI-driven development assistant.

Your responsibility is to continue this project safely, professionally, and incrementally.

The user must only execute complete PowerShell scripts.
Never ask the user to manually edit project files.

## Mandatory First Step

Before answering or generating development code, you MUST ask the user to provide the latest contents or generated report from:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

If file contents are not available in the chat, generate a PowerShell script that reads these files and prints or exports their content for the user to send back.

## Daily Workflow

### Step 1 - Read Project Knowledge

Analyze the following documentation:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

### Step 2 - Analyze Current State

Detect and report:

- architectural weaknesses
- technical debt
- missing modules
- missing infrastructure
- unclear requirements
- database risks
- SQLite multi-user risks
- security risks
- build risks
- installer and deployment risks
- UI/UX inconsistencies
- documentation inconsistencies

### Step 3 - Ask Clarification Questions

Before continuing development, ask the user important clarification questions when needed.

Questions should focus on:

- database schema design
- entity relationships
- domain rules
- personnel workflows
- task workflows
- leave calculation edge cases
- Persian calendar behavior
- 30-day calendar calculation behavior
- reminder engine behavior
- notification rules
- advanced logging strategy
- backup safety
- role and permission rules
- report and export design
- installer reliability

Do not continue coding if critical design decisions are still unclear.

### Step 4 - Update Documentation Automatically

Whenever the user answers clarification questions, update these files automatically through a complete PowerShell script:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md

Never wait for the user to remind you to update documentation.

### Step 5 - Development Rules

All development output must be ONE complete PowerShell script.

The script must:

- create folders if needed
- create or update files
- create or update C# code
- update documentation when needed
- run dotnet restore
- run dotnet build
- stop on errors
- avoid partial snippets

The user should only copy and paste one PowerShell script.

### Step 6 - Architectural Rules

Maintain this architecture:

- Platform: reusable common infrastructure
- Modules: feature modules
- Apps: WPF application

Keep dependency direction clean.

UI must not contain business logic.
Domain must not depend on WPF.
Infrastructure must not leak into UI.

### Step 7 - Data Access Decision

Use Entity Framework Core with SQLite.

Rules:

- Use EF Core SQLite provider
- Hide DbContext behind repositories/application services where appropriate
- Use UnitOfWork pattern where useful
- Use EF Core Change Tracking or Interceptors for advanced audit logging
- Enable safe SQLite settings for multi-user usage:
  - WAL mode
  - busy timeout
  - short transactions
  - retry handling where possible
- Avoid long-running write transactions

### Step 8 - Product Goal

Build a stable offline multi-user WPF desktop platform in Persian with:

- personnel management
- editable departments
- editable personnel statuses
- authentication and authorization
- checkbox-based permission management
- task management with steps
- multi-user task assignment
- task dependencies
- leave management
- Persian calendar
- normal and 30-day calculation mode per leave/reminder record
- official holidays
- admin-defined holidays
- reminder engine
- snooze/repeat/windows notification settings per reminder
- advanced audit logging
- reports
- Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export
- backup and restore
- simple manual update model
- professional Windows 11 style UI
- friendly Persian messages

### Step 9 - Session Output Format

Every session response must include:

1. Project understanding summary
2. Detected weaknesses and risks
3. Clarification questions, if needed
4. Proposed next safe development step
5. One complete PowerShell script, only when enough information exists
6. Reminder of what file/report the user should send next time

Wait for user confirmation before moving to the next phase.


``n---

# Architecture Decisions ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ 2026-06-16

These decisions were confirmed during architecture clarification.

## Decision 001 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Database Naming Convention
Chosen Option: **d**

## Decision 002 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Domain Entity Strategy
Chosen Option: **b**

## Decision 003 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Audit Logging Strategy
Chosen Model: **Hybrid Audit**

Meaning:
- Critical actions logged in database
- Technical logs stored in file logs
- Security events duplicated for safety

## Decision 004 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Task Status Design
Implementation: **TaskStatus Table**

Reason:
Task status must be configurable and extensible instead of hard-coded enum.

## Decision 005 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reminder System
Architecture: **Generic Reminder Engine**

Capabilities:
- Works with multiple modules
- Supports snooze / repeat
- Supports multiple notification channels

## Decision 006 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Import Architecture
Strategy: **ImportService per module**

Example:
- EmployeeImportService
- DepartmentImportService
- TaskImportService

Advantages:
- Clear validation rules
- Module isolation
- Easier maintenance

## Decision 007 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reporting Filters
Design:
**Dynamic Filter + Predefined Dataset**

Meaning:
- User-defined filters
- Prebuilt datasets for common reports
- Flexible reporting layer

## Decision 008 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Backup Configuration
Backup location controlled by:

**BackupLocation Setting**

Stored in:
System Settings table.

## Decision 009 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Architecture Choice
Chosen Option: **c**

## Decision 010 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Final Architecture Opinion

Recommended approach:

Hybrid enterprise desktop architecture combining:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- Metadata-driven reporting
- Dynamic field configuration
- Generic reminder engine
- RBAC security model

This ensures:

- maintainability
- scalability
- modular growth
- enterprise stability

## Decision 011 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Selected Option
Chosen Option: **a**

---


---


---

# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## 1. Architectural Intent

The project supports an extensibility model centered on:

- metadata-driven reporting
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven settings
- controlled customization per organization
- maintainable core architecture

The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.

---

## 2. Confirmed Dynamic Components

The roadmap already includes the following extensibility-related building blocks:

- EntityFieldSetting
- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

These components are used to support:

- field activation and visibility
- optional fields
- database-driven report definitions
- configurable report columns
- configurable report filters
- configurable report sorting

---

## 3. Customization Rules

Any customization must follow these rules:

1. **Database-driven first**
   - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.

2. **Core stability**
   - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.

3. **Explicit field control**
   - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.

4. **Report configuration separation**
   - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.

5. **Safe optional-field strategy**
   - Optional entity fields should follow the existing EmployeeOptionalFields approach when a structured extension is needed.

---

## 4. Dynamic Field Strategy

The project should use dynamic fields for cases where:

- a field may be enabled or disabled per deployment
- a field is optional for some organizations
- a field requires visibility control
- a field needs metadata-based behavior

The roadmap already confirms metadata-driven field activation and visibility, so this is the official direction for dynamic field behavior.

---

## 5. Dynamic Reporting Strategy

The reporting layer should remain metadata-driven.

That means reports can be defined using:

- a report definition
- selected columns
- filters
- sorting rules

This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

---

## 6. Field Settings Service

The presence of:

- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService

confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.

This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

---

## 7. Extensibility Boundary

This extensibility model is **not** a plugin framework.

It is a controlled metadata-driven customization approach for:

- field behavior
- report behavior
- optional structured data
- settings-based runtime decisions

No plugin architecture is introduced by this document unless explicitly added later in the SSOT.

---

## 8. Resulting Design Direction

The confirmed design direction is:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- metadata-driven customization
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven reporting metadata
- maintainable and enterprise-stable implementation

This keeps the system flexible while protecting the core codebase.

using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    public static IServiceProvider Services { get; private set; } = null!; = null!;

    protected override void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        var services = new ServiceCollection();

        ConfigureServices(services);

        Services = services.BuildServiceProvider();

        base.OnStartup(e);
    }

    private void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
    {
        // WPF Windows
        services.AddTransient<LoginWindow>();
        services.AddTransient<MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Presentation.Views.Dashboard.DashboardView>();
    }
}

---
using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;
using MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Auth;


namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    public static IServiceProvider? ServiceProvider { get; private set; }

    protected override void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        var services = new ServiceCollection();
        ConfigureServices(services);

        ServiceProvider = services.BuildServiceProvider();

        var loginWindow = ServiceProvider.GetRequiredService<LoginWindow>();
        loginWindow.Show();
    }

    private static void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
    {
        services.AddScoped<IAuthService, AuthService>();

        services.AddTransient<LoginWindow>();
        services.AddTransient<MainWindow>();
    }
}

## File: .maintenance_quarantine_stage2_20260619_175242__safety_backups_before_cleanup_20260619_130144_src_MyCompanyApp.Wpf_MainWindow.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\.maintenance_quarantine_stage2_20260619_175242__safety_backups_before_cleanup_20260619_130144_src_MyCompanyApp.Wpf_MainWindow.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}



---

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="720"
        Width="1220"
        MinHeight="650"
        MinWidth="1050"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\.maintenance_quarantine_stage2_20260619_175242__safety_backups_before_deep_cleanup_20260619_130018_src_MyCompanyApp.Wpf_MainWindow.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

## File: src_MyCompanyApp.Wpf_App.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\src_MyCompanyApp.Wpf_App.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Startup;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(System.Windows.StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
            .MinimumLevel.Debug()
            .Enrich.FromLogContext()
            .WriteTo.Console()
            .WriteTo.File("logs/app-.log", rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day)
            .CreateLogger();

        try
        {
            _host = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(e.Args)
                .UseSerilog()
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddApplicationServices(context.Configuration);
                })
                .Build();

            await _host.StartAsync();

            var mainWindow = _host.Services.GetRequiredService<MainWindow>();
            MainWindow = mainWindow;
            mainWindow.Show();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Fatal(ex, "Startup failed");
            System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                ex.ToString(),
                "Startup Error",
                System.Windows.MessageBoxButton.OK,
                System.Windows.MessageBoxImage.Error);
            Shutdown();
        }
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(System.Windows.ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host != null)
        {
            await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            _host.Dispose();
        }

        Log.CloseAndFlush();
        base.OnExit(e);
    }
}

## File: _backup_app_bootstrap_fix_20260627_173319_App.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\_backup_app_bootstrap_fix_20260627_173319_App.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using System.Windows.Threading;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using Serilog.Events;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class App : Application
    {
        private IHost? _host;

        public App()
        {
            ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling();
        }

        protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
        {
            base.OnStartup(e);

            try
            {
                EnsureRuntimeDirectories();

                _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();

                await _host.StartAsync().ConfigureAwait(true);

                Log.Information("Application host started successfully.");

                var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.GetServiceOrCreateInstance<MainWindow>(_host.Services);
                MainWindow = mainWindow;
                mainWindow.Show();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Fatal error during application startup.");

                MessageBox.Show(
                    "خطای بحرانی هنگام اجرای برنامه رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + ex.Message,
                    "MyCompanyApp - Startup Error",
                    MessageBoxButton.OK,
                    MessageBoxImage.Error);

                Shutdown(-1);
            }
        }

        protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
        {
            try
            {
                if (_host is not null)
                {
                    Log.Information("Stopping application host...");
                    await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5)).ConfigureAwait(true);
                    _host.Dispose();
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Error(ex, "Error while stopping application host.");
            }
            finally
            {
                Log.Information("Application exited with code {ExitCode}.", e.ApplicationExitCode);
                Log.CloseAndFlush();
                base.OnExit(e);
            }
        }

        public static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[]? args)
        {
            return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args ?? Array.Empty<string>())
                .UseContentRoot(AppContext.BaseDirectory)
                .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
                {
                    config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);

                    config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                    config.AddJsonFile(
                        $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                        optional: true,
                        reloadOnChange: true);

                    config.AddEnvironmentVariables(prefix: "MYCOMPANYAPP_");

                    if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                    {
                        config.AddCommandLine(args);
                    }
                })
                .UseSerilog((context, services, loggerConfiguration) =>
                {
                    loggerConfiguration
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("Microsoft", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("System", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .ReadFrom.Configuration(context.Configuration)
                        .ReadFrom.Services(services)
                        .Enrich.FromLogContext()
                        .WriteTo.Debug()
                        .WriteTo.File(
                            path: Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs", "app-.log"),
                            rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day,
                            retainedFileCountLimit: 31,
                            shared: true);
                })
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddOptions();
                    services.AddLogging();

                    services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();

                    // Phase 1 extension point:
                    // Register Application/Infrastructure/Persistence/Platform services here
                    // or via extension methods in future phases.
                });
        }

        private static void EnsureRuntimeDirectories()
        {
            Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs"));
        }

        private void ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling()
        {
            DispatcherUnhandledException += OnDispatcherUnhandledException;
            AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += OnAppDomainUnhandledException;
            TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += OnUnobservedTaskException;
        }

        private void OnDispatcherUnhandledException(object sender, DispatcherUnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unhandled UI dispatcher exception.");

            MessageBox.Show(
                "یک خطای پیش‌بینی‌نشده در رابط کاربری رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + e.Exception.Message,
                "MyCompanyApp - UI Error",
                MessageBoxButton.OK,
                MessageBoxImage.Error);

            e.Handled = true;
        }

        private static void OnAppDomainUnhandledException(object sender, UnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            if (e.ExceptionObject is Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Unhandled AppDomain exception. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.IsTerminating);
            }
            else
            {
                Log.Fatal("Unhandled AppDomain exception object: {ExceptionObject}. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.ExceptionObject, e.IsTerminating);
            }
        }

        private static void OnUnobservedTaskException(object? sender, UnobservedTaskExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unobserved task exception.");
            e.SetObserved();
        }
    }
}


---

                    services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();

                    // or via extension methods in future phases.
                });
        }

        private static void EnsureRuntimeDirectories()
        {

        }

        private void ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling()
        {
            DispatcherUnhandledException += OnDispatcherUnhandledException;
            AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += OnAppDomainUnhandledException;
            TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += OnUnobservedTaskException;
        }

        private void OnDispatcherUnhandledException(object sender, DispatcherUnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {

                "MyCompanyApp - UI Error",

## File: _backup_bootstrap_fix_20260627_175034_App.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\_backup_bootstrap_fix_20260627_175034_App.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using MainWindowView = MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers();

        _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();
        await _host.StartAsync();

        var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance<MainWindowView>(_host.Services);
        MainWindow = mainWindow;
        mainWindow.Show();
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host is not null)
        {
            try
            {
                await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            }
            finally
            {
                _host.Dispose();
            }
        }

        base.OnExit(e);
    }

    private static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[] args)
    {
        return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
            .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
            {
                config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);
                config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddJsonFile(
                    $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                    optional: true,
                    reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddEnvironmentVariables();

                if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                {
                    config.AddCommandLine(args);
                }
            })
            .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
            {
                services.AddTransient<MainWindowView>();
            })
            .ConfigureLogging(logging =>
            {
                logging.ClearProviders();
                logging.AddConsole();
                logging.AddDebug();
            });
    }

    private void RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers()
    {
        DispatcherUnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.Handled = true;
        };

        AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            if (args.ExceptionObject is Exception exception)
            {
                ShowFatalError(exception);
            }
        };

        TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.SetObserved();
        };
    }

    private static void ShowFatalError(Exception exception)
    {
        MessageBox.Show(
            exception.ToString(),
            "Unexpected application error",
            MessageBoxButton.OK,
            MessageBoxImage.Error);
    }
}



---

## File: _backup_bootstrap_fix_20260627_175034_App.xaml.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\_backup_bootstrap_fix_20260627_175034_App.xaml.txt
---

## File: _backup_phase1_20260627_174935_App.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\_backup_phase1_20260627_174935_App.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using MainWindowView = MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers();

        _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();
        await _host.StartAsync();

        var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance<MainWindowView>(_host.Services);
        MainWindow = mainWindow;
        mainWindow.Show();
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host is not null)
        {
            try
            {
                await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            }
            finally
            {
                _host.Dispose();
            }
        }

        base.OnExit(e);
    }

    private static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[] args)
    {
        return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
            .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
            {
                config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);
                config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddJsonFile(
                    $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                    optional: true,
                    reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddEnvironmentVariables();

                if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                {
                    config.AddCommandLine(args);
                }
            })
            .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
            {
                services.AddTransient<MainWindowView>();
            })
            .ConfigureLogging(logging =>
            {
                logging.ClearProviders();
                logging.AddConsole();
                logging.AddDebug();
            });
    }

    private void RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers()
    {
        DispatcherUnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.Handled = true;
        };

        AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            if (args.ExceptionObject is Exception exception)
            {
                ShowFatalError(exception);
            }
        };

        TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.SetObserved();
        };
    }

    private static void ShowFatalError(Exception exception)
    {
        MessageBox.Show(
            exception.ToString(),
            "Unexpected application error",
            MessageBoxButton.OK,
            MessageBoxImage.Error);
    }
}

## File: _backup_phase1_20260627_174935_MainWindow.xaml.cs.txt
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\_ExtractedShellFiles\_backup_phase1_20260627_174935_MainWindow.xaml.cs.txt
---
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}
