<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="720"
        Width="1220"
        MinHeight="650"
        MinWidth="1050"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

using System;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Startup;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(System.Windows.StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
            .MinimumLevel.Debug()
            .Enrich.FromLogContext()
            .WriteTo.Console()
            .WriteTo.File("logs/app-.log", rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day)
            .CreateLogger();

        try
        {
            _host = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(e.Args)
                .UseSerilog()
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddApplicationServices(context.Configuration);
                })
                .Build();

            await _host.StartAsync();

            var mainWindow = _host.Services.GetRequiredService<MainWindow>();
            MainWindow = mainWindow;
            mainWindow.Show();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Fatal(ex, "Startup failed");
            System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                ex.ToString(),
                "Startup Error",
                System.Windows.MessageBoxButton.OK,
                System.Windows.MessageBoxImage.Error);
            Shutdown();
        }
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(System.Windows.ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host != null)
        {
            await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            _host.Dispose();
        }

        Log.CloseAndFlush();
        base.OnExit(e);
    }
}

            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        }
    }
}

# ARCHITECTURE

Generated: 2026-06-27 14:42:16

## Baseline
The solution follows a modular layered WPF architecture with a dedicated shell project.

## Core Layers
- src/MyCompanyApp.Domain — domain model and rules
- src/MyCompanyApp.Application — use-cases and application services
- src/MyCompanyApp.Persistence — persistence and data access
- src/MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure — external/infrastructure implementations

using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    public static IServiceProvider Services { get; private set; } = null!; = null!;

    protected override void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        var services = new ServiceCollection();

        ConfigureServices(services);

        Services = services.BuildServiceProvider();

        base.OnStartup(e);
    }

    private void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
    {
        // WPF Windows
        services.AddTransient<LoginWindow>();
        services.AddTransient<MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Presentation.Views.Dashboard.DashboardView>();
    }
}


----- END FILE CONTENT: .maintenance\phase4_fix_20260620_125052\App.xaml.cs -----

using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Interfaces.Auth;
using MyCompanyApp.Infrastructure.Services.Auth;


namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    public static IServiceProvider? ServiceProvider { get; private set; }

    protected override void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        var services = new ServiceCollection();
        ConfigureServices(services);

        ServiceProvider = services.BuildServiceProvider();

        var loginWindow = ServiceProvider.GetRequiredService<LoginWindow>();
        loginWindow.Show();
    }

    private static void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
    {
        services.AddScoped<IAuthService, AuthService>();

        services.AddTransient<LoginWindow>();
        services.AddTransient<MainWindow>();
    }
}

----- END FILE CONTENT: .maintenance\quarantine\duplicate-cleanup\20260620_171944\DIR__backup_auth_login_fix_20260620_170331\src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\App.xaml.cs -----

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="720"
        Width="1220"
        MinHeight="650"
        MinWidth="1050"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

FILE: _backup_app_bootstrap_fix_20260627_173319\App.xaml.cs
SOURCE: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\_backup_app_bootstrap_fix_20260627_173319\App.xaml.cs

----- BEGIN FILE CONTENT: _backup_app_bootstrap_fix_20260627_173319\App.xaml.cs -----
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using System.Windows.Threading;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using Serilog.Events;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class App : Application
    {
        private IHost? _host;

        public App()
        {
            ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling();
        }

        protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
        {
            base.OnStartup(e);

            try
            {
                EnsureRuntimeDirectories();

                _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();

                await _host.StartAsync().ConfigureAwait(true);

                Log.Information("Application host started successfully.");

                var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.GetServiceOrCreateInstance<MainWindow>(_host.Services);
                MainWindow = mainWindow;
                mainWindow.Show();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Fatal error during application startup.");

                MessageBox.Show(
                    "خطای بحرانی هنگام اجرای برنامه رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + ex.Message,
                    "MyCompanyApp - Startup Error",
                    MessageBoxButton.OK,
                    MessageBoxImage.Error);

                Shutdown(-1);
            }
        }

        protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
        {
            try
            {
                if (_host is not null)
                {
                    Log.Information("Stopping application host...");
                    await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5)).ConfigureAwait(true);
                    _host.Dispose();
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Error(ex, "Error while stopping application host.");
            }
            finally
            {
                Log.Information("Application exited with code {ExitCode}.", e.ApplicationExitCode);
                Log.CloseAndFlush();
                base.OnExit(e);
            }
        }

        public static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[]? args)
        {
            return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args ?? Array.Empty<string>())
                .UseContentRoot(AppContext.BaseDirectory)
                .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
                {
                    config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);

                    config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                    config.AddJsonFile(
                        $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                        optional: true,
                        reloadOnChange: true);

                    config.AddEnvironmentVariables(prefix: "MYCOMPANYAPP_");

                    if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                    {
                        config.AddCommandLine(args);
                    }
                })
                .UseSerilog((context, services, loggerConfiguration) =>
                {
                    loggerConfiguration
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("Microsoft", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("System", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .ReadFrom.Configuration(context.Configuration)
                        .ReadFrom.Services(services)
                        .Enrich.FromLogContext()
                        .WriteTo.Debug()
                        .WriteTo.File(
                            path: Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs", "app-.log"),
                            rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day,
                            retainedFileCountLimit: 31,
                            shared: true);
                })
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddOptions();
                    services.AddLogging();

                    services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();

                    // Phase 1 extension point:
                    // Register Application/Infrastructure/Persistence/Platform services here
                    // or via extension methods in future phases.
                });
        }

        private static void EnsureRuntimeDirectories()
        {
            Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs"));
        }

        private void ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling()
        {
            DispatcherUnhandledException += OnDispatcherUnhandledException;
            AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += OnAppDomainUnhandledException;
            TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += OnUnobservedTaskException;
        }

        private void OnDispatcherUnhandledException(object sender, DispatcherUnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unhandled UI dispatcher exception.");

            MessageBox.Show(
                "یک خطای پیش‌بینی‌نشده در رابط کاربری رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + e.Exception.Message,
                "MyCompanyApp - UI Error",
                MessageBoxButton.OK,
                MessageBoxImage.Error);

            e.Handled = true;
        }

        private static void OnAppDomainUnhandledException(object sender, UnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            if (e.ExceptionObject is Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Unhandled AppDomain exception. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.IsTerminating);
            }
            else
            {
                Log.Fatal("Unhandled AppDomain exception object: {ExceptionObject}. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.ExceptionObject, e.IsTerminating);
            }
        }

        private static void OnUnobservedTaskException(object? sender, UnobservedTaskExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unobserved task exception.");
            e.SetObserved();
        }
    }
}

using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using System.Windows.Threading;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using Serilog.Events;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class App : Application
    {
        private IHost? _host;

        public App()
        {
            ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling();
        }

        protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
        {
            base.OnStartup(e);

            try
            {
                EnsureRuntimeDirectories();

                _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();

                await _host.StartAsync().ConfigureAwait(true);

                Log.Information("Application host started successfully.");

                var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.GetServiceOrCreateInstance<MainWindow>(_host.Services);
                MainWindow = mainWindow;
                mainWindow.Show();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Fatal error during application startup.");

                MessageBox.Show(
                    "خطای بحرانی هنگام اجرای برنامه رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + ex.Message,
                    "MyCompanyApp - Startup Error",
                    MessageBoxButton.OK,
                    MessageBoxImage.Error);

                Shutdown(-1);
            }
        }

        protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
        {
            try
            {
                if (_host is not null)
                {
                    Log.Information("Stopping application host...");
                    await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5)).ConfigureAwait(true);
                    _host.Dispose();
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Error(ex, "Error while stopping application host.");
            }
            finally
            {
                Log.Information("Application exited with code {ExitCode}.", e.ApplicationExitCode);
                Log.CloseAndFlush();
                base.OnExit(e);
            }
        }

        public static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[]? args)
        {
            return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args ?? Array.Empty<string>())
                .UseContentRoot(AppContext.BaseDirectory)
                .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
                {
                    config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);

                    config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                    config.AddJsonFile(
                        $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                        optional: true,
                        reloadOnChange: true);

                    config.AddEnvironmentVariables(prefix: "MYCOMPANYAPP_");

                    if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                    {
                        config.AddCommandLine(args);
                    }
                })
                .UseSerilog((context, services, loggerConfiguration) =>
                {
                    loggerConfiguration
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("Microsoft", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .MinimumLevel.Override("System", LogEventLevel.Warning)
                        .ReadFrom.Configuration(context.Configuration)
                        .ReadFrom.Services(services)
                        .Enrich.FromLogContext()
                        .WriteTo.Debug()
                        .WriteTo.File(
                            path: Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs", "app-.log"),
                            rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day,
                            retainedFileCountLimit: 31,
                            shared: true);
                })
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddOptions();
                    services.AddLogging();

                    services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();

                    // Phase 1 extension point:
                    // Register Application/Infrastructure/Persistence/Platform services here
                    // or via extension methods in future phases.
                });
        }

        private static void EnsureRuntimeDirectories()
        {
            Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs"));
        }

        private void ConfigureGlobalExceptionHandling()
        {
            DispatcherUnhandledException += OnDispatcherUnhandledException;
            AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += OnAppDomainUnhandledException;
            TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += OnUnobservedTaskException;
        }

        private void OnDispatcherUnhandledException(object sender, DispatcherUnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unhandled UI dispatcher exception.");

            MessageBox.Show(
                "یک خطای پیش‌بینی‌نشده در رابط کاربری رخ داد." + Environment.NewLine + Environment.NewLine + e.Exception.Message,
                "MyCompanyApp - UI Error",
                MessageBoxButton.OK,
                MessageBoxImage.Error);

            e.Handled = true;
        }

        private static void OnAppDomainUnhandledException(object sender, UnhandledExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            if (e.ExceptionObject is Exception ex)
            {
                Log.Fatal(ex, "Unhandled AppDomain exception. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.IsTerminating);
            }
            else
            {
                Log.Fatal("Unhandled AppDomain exception object: {ExceptionObject}. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}", e.ExceptionObject, e.IsTerminating);
            }
        }

        private static void OnUnobservedTaskException(object? sender, UnobservedTaskExceptionEventArgs e)
        {
            Log.Error(e.Exception, "Unobserved task exception.");
            e.SetObserved();
        }
    }
}

using System;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using MainWindowView = MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        base.OnStartup(e);

        RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers();

        _host = CreateHostBuilder(e.Args).Build();
        await _host.StartAsync();

        var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance<MainWindowView>(_host.Services);
        MainWindow = mainWindow;
        mainWindow.Show();
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host is not null)
        {
            try
            {
                await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            }
            finally
            {
                _host.Dispose();
            }
        }

        base.OnExit(e);
    }

    private static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[] args)
    {
        return Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
            .ConfigureAppConfiguration((context, config) =>
            {
                config.SetBasePath(AppContext.BaseDirectory);
                config.AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddJsonFile(
                    $"appsettings.{context.HostingEnvironment.EnvironmentName}.json",
                    optional: true,
                    reloadOnChange: true);
                config.AddEnvironmentVariables();

                if (args is { Length: > 0 })
                {
                    config.AddCommandLine(args);
                }
            })
            .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
            {
                services.AddTransient<MainWindowView>();
            })
            .ConfigureLogging(logging =>
            {
                logging.ClearProviders();
                logging.AddConsole();
                logging.AddDebug();
            });
    }

    private void RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers()
    {
        DispatcherUnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.Handled = true;
        };

        AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += (_, args) =>
        {
            if (args.ExceptionObject is Exception exception)
            {
                ShowFatalError(exception);
            }
        };

        TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += (_, args) =>
        {
            ShowFatalError(args.Exception);
            args.SetObserved();
        };
    }

    private static void ShowFatalError(Exception exception)
    {
        MessageBox.Show(
            exception.ToString(),
            "Unexpected application error",
            MessageBoxButton.OK,
            MessageBoxImage.Error);
    }
}

using System.Threading.Tasks;

using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using MainWindowView = MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(StartupEventArgs e)

        await _host.StartAsync();

        var mainWindow = ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance<MainWindowView>(_host.Services);

                await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            }

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="720"
        Width="1220"
        MinHeight="650"
        MinWidth="1050"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        Background="#F8FAFC"
        FlowDirection="RightToLeft">

    <Grid>
        <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="260"/>
            <ColumnDefinition Width="*"/>
        </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>

        <Border Grid.Column="0"
                Background="#0F172A">
            <Grid Margin="0">
                <Grid.RowDefinitions>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="*"/>
                    <RowDefinition Height="Auto"/>
                </Grid.RowDefinitions>

                <StackPanel Margin="20,24,20,18">
                    <TextBlock Text="MyCompanyApp"
                               Foreground="White"
                               FontSize="23"
                               FontWeight="Bold"/>

                    <TextBlock Text="سامانه مدیریتی شرکت"
                               Margin="0,8,0,0"
                               Foreground="#94A3B8"
                               FontSize="13"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Margin="14,8,14,0">
                    <Button x:Name="DashboardBtn"
                            Content="داشبورد"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="DashboardBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="UsersBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت کاربران"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="UsersBtn_Click"/>

                    <Button x:Name="LeaveBtn"
                            Content="مدیریت مرخصی"
                            Height="44"
                            Margin="0,5"
                            Click="LeaveBtn_Click"/>
                </StackPanel>

                <TextBlock Grid.Row="2"
                           Text="Enterprise Modular WPF"
                           Margin="20"
                           Foreground="#64748B"
                           FontSize="12"/>
            </Grid>
        </Border>

        <Border Grid.Column="1"
                Background="#F8FAFC">
            <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent"/>
        </Border>
    </Grid>
</Window>

using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

using System;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Startup;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(System.Windows.StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
            .MinimumLevel.Debug()
            .Enrich.FromLogContext()
            .WriteTo.Console()
            .WriteTo.File("logs/app-.log", rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day)
            .CreateLogger();

        try
        {
            _host = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(e.Args)
                .UseSerilog()
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddApplicationServices(context.Configuration);
                })
                .Build();

            await _host.StartAsync();

            var mainWindow = _host.Services.GetRequiredService<MainWindow>();
            MainWindow = mainWindow;
            mainWindow.Show();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Fatal(ex, "Startup failed");
            System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                ex.ToString(),
                "Startup Error",
                System.Windows.MessageBoxButton.OK,
                System.Windows.MessageBoxImage.Error);
            Shutdown();
        }
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(System.Windows.ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host != null)
        {
            await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            _host.Dispose();
        }

        Log.CloseAndFlush();
        base.OnExit(e);
    }
}

<Window x:Class="MyCompanyApp.Wpf.MainWindow"
        xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Views"
        Title="MyCompanyApp"
        Height="450"
        Width="800">
    <Grid>
        <ContentControl x:Name="MainContent">
            <views:UsersView/>
        </ContentControl>

----- BEGIN FILE CONTENT: src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\MainWindow.xaml.cs -----
using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

----- END FILE CONTENT: src\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\MainWindow.xaml.cs -----

using System;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
using Serilog;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Startup;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf;

public partial class App : System.Windows.Application
{
    private IHost? _host;

    protected override async void OnStartup(System.Windows.StartupEventArgs e)
    {
        Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
            .MinimumLevel.Debug()
            .Enrich.FromLogContext()
            .WriteTo.Console()
            .WriteTo.File("logs/app-.log", rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day)
            .CreateLogger();

        try
        {
            _host = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(e.Args)
                .UseSerilog()
                .ConfigureServices((context, services) =>
                {
                    services.AddApplicationServices(context.Configuration);
                })
                .Build();

            await _host.StartAsync();

            var mainWindow = _host.Services.GetRequiredService<MainWindow>();
            MainWindow = mainWindow;
            mainWindow.Show();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Fatal(ex, "Startup failed");
            System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                ex.ToString(),
                "Startup Error",
                System.Windows.MessageBoxButton.OK,
                System.Windows.MessageBoxImage.Error);
            Shutdown();
        }
    }

    protected override async void OnExit(System.Windows.ExitEventArgs e)
    {
        if (_host != null)
        {
            await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            _host.Dispose();
        }

        Log.CloseAndFlush();
        base.OnExit(e);
    }
}

using System.Windows;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users.Views;
using MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave.Views;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf
{
    public partial class MainWindow : Window
    {
        private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;

        public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            _navigationService = new NavigationService();
            _navigationService.AttachHost(MainContent);

            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void DashboardBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            NavigateToDashboard();
        }

        private void UsersBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new UsersView(), "users");
        }

        private void LeaveBtn_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new LeaveView(), "leave");
        }

        private void NavigateToDashboard()
        {
            _navigationService.Navigate(new DashboardView(), "dashboard");
        }
    }
}

using System;
using System.Windows;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Presentation.Windows;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Services.Navigation;

public sealed class NavigationService : INavigationService
{
    private readonly IServiceProvider _serviceProvider;

    public NavigationService(IServiceProvider serviceProvider)
    {
        _serviceProvider = serviceProvider;
    }

    public void ShowDashboard(Window? currentWindow = null)
    {
        var dashboardWindow = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<DashboardWindow>();
        System.Windows.Application.Current.MainWindow = dashboardWindow;
        dashboardWindow.Show();
        currentWindow?.Close();
    }
}

using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using MyCompanyApp.Persistence;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.Core.Errors;
using MyCompanyApp.Platform.Core.Services;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Startup;

public static class DependencyInjectionExtensions
{
    public static IServiceCollection AddApplicationServices(this IServiceCollection services, IConfiguration configuration)
    {
        services.AddPersistence(configuration);
        services.AddErrorHandling();
        services.AddBaseServices();
        services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>();

        return services;
    }
}

First 120 lines preview:
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Services;
using System.Windows.Input;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Commands;
using MyCompanyApp.Application.Core;
using MyCompanyApp.Wpf.Services;

namespace MyCompanyApp.Wpf.ViewModels;

public sealed class MainViewModel : BaseViewModel
{
    private readonly INavigationService _navigationService;

    public MainViewModel(INavigationService navigationService)
    {
        _navigationService = navigationService;
        NavigateHomeCommand = new RelayCommand(NavigateHome);
    }

    public BaseViewModel CurrentViewModel => _navigationService.CurrentViewModel;

    public ICommand NavigateHomeCommand { get; }

    private void NavigateHome()
    {
        _navigationService.NavigateTo<HomeViewModel>();
        OnPropertyChanged(nameof(CurrentViewModel));
    }
}

## File: ARCHITECTURE.md
Location: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf\docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
---
# MyCompanyApp Architecture

## Technology Stack

- .NET 8/9
- WPF
- Dependency Injection
- Generic Host
- Modular Architecture

## Application Layers

### Shell
- MainWindow
- ShellViewModel

### Navigation
- NavigationService
- NavigationRouteRegistry

### Modules

- bin Module
- Commands Module
- Debug Module
- DependencyInjection Module
- DTOs Module
- MyCompanyApp.Modules.Dashboard Module
- MyCompanyApp.Modules.Leave Module
- MyCompanyApp.Modules.Reports Module
- MyCompanyApp.Modules.Users Module
- net8.0-windows Module
- obj Module
- Queries Module
- ref Module
- refint Module
- Services Module
- ViewModels Module
- Views Module

### Dependency Injection Flow

1. App.xaml.cs starts Generic Host
2. Services registered in DI container
3. Modules register Views and Services
4. Navigation routes registered
5. ShellViewModel navigates to default route

    1: using System.ComponentModel;
    2: using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;
    3: 
    4: namespace MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation
    5: {
    6:     public sealed class NavigationHostViewModel : INotifyPropertyChanged
    7:     {
    8:         private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;
    9:         private object? _currentView;
   10: 
   11:         public NavigationHostViewModel(NavigationService navigationService)
   12:         {
   13:             _navigationService = navigationService;
   14:             _currentView = navigationService.CurrentView;
   15: 
   16:             _navigationService.Navigated += (_, args) =>
   17:             {
   18:                 CurrentView = args.View;
   19:             };
   20:         }
   21: 
   22:         public object? CurrentView
   23:         {
   24:             get => _currentView;
   25:             private set
   26:             {
   27:                 if (!ReferenceEquals(_currentView, value))
   28:                 {
   29:                     _currentView = value;
   30:                     OnPropertyChanged();
   31:                 }
   32:             }
   33:         }
   34: 
   35:         public event PropertyChangedEventHandler? PropertyChanged;
   36: 
   37:         private void OnPropertyChanged([CallerMemberName] string? propertyName = null)
   38:         {
   39:             PropertyChanged?.Invoke(this, new PropertyChangedEventArgs(propertyName));
   40:         }
   41:     }
   42: }

    1: using System;
    2: 
    3: using System.Windows.Controls;
    4: 
    5: using MyCompanyApp.Platform.Core.Navigation;
    6: 
    7: using System.Collections.Generic;
    8: 
    9: using System.Linq;
   10: 
   11: using MyCompanyApp.Application.Modules;
   12: 
   13: using MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
   14: 
   15: 
   16: 
   17: namespace MyCompanyApp.Platform.UI.Navigation;
   18: 
   19: 
   20: 
   21: public sealed class MainNavigationService
   22: 
   23: {
   24: 
   25:     private readonly IModuleCatalog _moduleCatalog;
   26: 
   27:     private readonly NavigationService _navigationService;
   28: 
   29:     private readonly IViewFactory _IViewFactory;
   30: 
   31: 
   32: 
   33:     public MainNavigationService(
   34: 
   35:     IModuleCatalog moduleCatalog,
   36: 
   37:     NavigationService navigationService,
   38: 
   39:     IViewFactory IViewFactory)
   40: 
   41:     {
   42: 
   43:     _moduleCatalog = moduleCatalog;
   44: 
   45:     _navigationService = navigationService;
   46: 
   47:     _IViewFactory = IViewFactory;
   48: 
   49:     }
   50: 
   51: 
   52: 
   53:     public IReadOnlyList<NavigationItemViewModel> GetNavigationItems()
   54: 
   55:     {
   56: 
   57:         return _moduleCatalog
   58: 
   59:             .GetAvailableModules()
   60: 
   61:             .OrderBy(x => x.SortOrder)
   62: 
   63:             .Select(module => new NavigationItemViewModel(
   64: 
   65:                 module.Id,
   66: 
   67:                 module.DisplayName,
   68: 
   69:                 module.Id,
   70: 
   71:                 module.RequiredPermission ?? string.Empty,
   72: 
   73:                 module.Icon ?? string.Empty,
   74: 
   75:                 () => _navigationService.Navigate(ToUserControl(_IViewFactory.Create(module.Id)))))
   76: 
   77:             .ToList();
   78: 
   79:     }
   80: 
   81: 
   82: 
   83:     public void NavigateToDefault()
   84: 
   85:     {
   86: 
   87:         var firstModule = _moduleCatalog
   88: 
   89:             .GetAvailableModules()
   90: 
   91:             .OrderBy(x => x.SortOrder)
   92: 
   93:             .FirstOrDefault();
   94: 
   95: 
   96: 
   97:         if (firstModule is null)
   98: 
   99:             return;
  100: 
  101: 
  102: 
  103:         _navigationService.Navigate(ToUserControl(_IViewFactory.Create(firstModule.Id)));
  104: 
  105:     }
  106: 
  107: 
  108: 
  109:     
  110:     public IReadOnlyList<NavigationItemViewModel> GetItems()
  111:     {
  112:         return GetNavigationItems();
  113:     }
  114: private static UserControl ToUserControl(object view)
  115: 
  116:     {
  117: 
  118:         if (view is UserControl userControl)
  119: 
  120:         {
  121: 
  122:             return userControl;
  123: 
  124:         }
  125: 
  126: 
  127: 
  128:         throw new InvalidOperationException("ViewFactory returned an invalid view instance. Expected System.Windows.Controls.UserControl, but got " + (view?.GetType().FullName ?? "null") + ".");
  129: 
  130:     }
  131: 
  132: }
  133: 
  134: 
  135:

   18:     {

   21:         dashboardWindow.Show();
   22:         currentWindow?.Close();

 2415: 
 2416: The project supports an extensibility model centered on:
 2417:

 2419: - dynamic field configuration
 2420: - database-driven settings
 2421: - controlled customization per organization

 2423: 
 2424: The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.
 2425:

 2452: ## 3. Customization Rules
 2453: 
 2454: Any customization must follow these rules:
 2455: 
 2456: 1. **Database-driven first**
 2457:    - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.
 2458: 
 2459: 2. **Core stability**
 2460:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 2461: 
 2462: 3. **Explicit field control**

-- hit at line 2463 --
 2458: 
 2459: 2. **Core stability**
 2460:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 2461: 
 2462: 3. **Explicit field control**
 2463:    - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.
 2464: 
 2465: 4. **Report configuration separation**
 2466:    - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.
 2467: 
 2468: 5. **Safe optional-field strategy**

-- hit at line 2501 --
 2496: 
 2497: This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.
 2498:

 6015: ## 3. Customization Rules
 6016: 
 6017: Any customization must follow these rules:
 6018: 
 6019: 1. **Database-driven first**
 6020:    - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.
 6021: 
 6022: 2. **Core stability**
 6023:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 6024: 
 6025: 3. **Explicit field control**

-- hit at line 6026 --
 6021: 
 6022: 2. **Core stability**
 6023:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 6024: 
 6025: 3. **Explicit field control**
 6026:    - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.
 6027: 
 6028: 4. **Report configuration separation**
 6029:    - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.
 6030: 
 6031: 5. **Safe optional-field strategy**

-- hit at line 6064 --
 6059: 
 6060: This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

 9110: ## 3. Customization Rules
 9111: 
 9112: Any customization must follow these rules:
 9113: 
 9114: 1. **Database-driven first**
 9115:    - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.
 9116: 
 9117: 2. **Core stability**
 9118:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 9119: 
 9120: 3. **Explicit field control**

-- hit at line 9121 --
 9116: 
 9117: 2. **Core stability**
 9118:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
 9119: 
 9120: 3. **Explicit field control**
 9121:    - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.
 9122: 
 9123: 4. **Report configuration separation**
 9124:    - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.
 9125: 
 9126: 5. **Safe optional-field strategy**

-- hit at line 9159 --
 9154: 
 9155: This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.
 9156:

 9162:

 9159: ## 6. Field Settings Service

 9164: - EntityFieldSettingsService
 9165: 
 9166: confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.
 9167: 
 9168: This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

 9160: 
 9161: The presence of:
 9162:

 9164: - EntityFieldSettingsService

 9167:

 9177:

11010: 1. Application installs and runs through EXE installer.

14830: 
14831: The project supports an extensibility model centered on:
14832:

14834: - dynamic field configuration
14835: - database-driven settings
14836: - controlled customization per organization
14837: - maintainable core architecture
14838: 
14839: The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.
14840:

14867: ## 3. Customization Rules
14868: 
14869: Any customization must follow these rules:
14870: 
14871: 1. **Database-driven first**
14872:    - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.
14873: 
14874: 2. **Core stability**
14875:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
14876: 
14877: 3. **Explicit field control**

-- hit at line 14878 --
14873: 
14874: 2. **Core stability**
14875:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
14876: 
14877: 3. **Explicit field control**
14878:    - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.
14879: 
14880: 4. **Report configuration separation**
14881:    - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.
14882: 
14883: 5. **Safe optional-field strategy**

-- hit at line 14916 --
14911: 
14912: This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

16074: ## 3. Customization Rules
16075: 
16076: Any customization must follow these rules:
16077: 
16078: 1. **Database-driven first**
16079:    - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.
16080: 
16081: 2. **Core stability**
16082:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
16083: 
16084: 3. **Explicit field control**

-- hit at line 16085 --
16080: 
16081: 2. **Core stability**
16082:    - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.
16083: 
16084: 3. **Explicit field control**
16085:    - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.
16086: 
16087: 4. **Report configuration separation**
16088:    - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.
16089: 
16090: 5. **Safe optional-field strategy**

16118: 
16119: This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

-- hit at line 4196 --
 4191: 
 4192: ### Migration Policy
 4193: 1. Schema changes must be handled through EF Core migrations.
 4194: 2. Dynamic metadata changes must be stored in database tables.
 4195: 3. V1 does not support automatic execution of unknown third-party migrations.
 4196: 4. Developer-created modules may add migrations as part of normal application releases.
 4197: 
 4198: ### Versioning
 4199: Each internal module should expose a module version for diagnostics and future upgrade paths.
 4200: 
 4201: ### Explicit Non-Goals for V1

13589: ## 18.4 Customization Principles
13590: 

-- hit at line 13594 --
13589: ## 18.4 Customization Principles
13590: 
13591: All customization must follow these rules:
13592: 
13593: 1. **Protect the core architecture**
13594:    - Core modules must stay clean and stable.
13595:    - Custom behavior should extend the platform, not damage it.
13596: 
13597: 2. **Prefer metadata over hardcoding**
13598:    - Use configuration tables and definitions whenever possible.
13599: 

-- hit at line 13608 --
13603: 
13604: 4. **Preserve upgradeability**
13605:    - Future updates must remain possible without destroying custom client data.
13606: 
13607: 5. **Use explicit extension points**
13608:    - Custom modules, forms, and tables must have clear registration and ownership.
13609: 
13610: ---
13611: 
13612: ## 18.5 Metadata-Driven Design
13613: 

-- hit at line 13664 --
13659: 
13660: ## 18.7 Dynamic Form Strategy
13661: 
13662: The UI must support adding new forms without modifying the shell architecture.
13663: 
13664: Each module should be able to register:
13665: - its views
13666: - its view models
13667: - its commands
13668: - its navigation items
13669: - its validation rules

# MyCompanyApp.Wpf Development Roadmap

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:46

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 1 â€” CORE INFRASTRUCTURE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Establish strong architecture foundation.

Tasks

Setup dependency injection
Create configuration management
Create logging infrastructure
Create error handling framework
Create base services
Ensure application layer testability

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 2 â€” DATABASE & PERSISTENCE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Reliable and optimized database layer.

Tasks

Database context design
Entity configuration
Index strategy
Performance optimization
Migration system
Database health monitoring

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 3 â€” AUTHENTICATION & USER MANAGEMENT
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Secure user authentication and authorization.

Features

Professional Login Form
Password hashing
User management panel
Role-based access control (RBAC)
Permission system for application sections

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 4 â€” MODERN UI FRAMEWORK
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Create modern and attractive UI.

Features

Professional Dashboard
Custom message dialog system
Theme switching (Light / Dark / Custom)
Font switching
Reusable UI components
Smooth UI interactions

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 5 â€” DASHBOARD SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Provide quick insight into system status.

Features

Statistics widgets
Charts
Activity overview
Quick navigation panels

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 6 â€” REMINDER SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Provide internal reminder system.

Features

Time-based reminders
Notification popup
Recurring reminders
Reminder management panel

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 7 â€” LEAVE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Employee leave tracking.

Features

Leave request submission
Leave approval workflow
Leave history
Leave reporting

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 8 â€” REPORTING ENGINE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Dynamic reporting capabilities.

Features

Dynamic report builder
Data filtering
Interactive reports
Chart-based reports

Export capabilities

Excel export
PDF export
PowerPoint export for presentation reports

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 9 â€” BACKUP SYSTEM
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Data safety.

Features

Manual backup
Scheduled backup
Restore functionality
Backup compression

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 10 â€” PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Ensure fast application performance.

Tasks

Database query optimization
Async processing
Memory management
UI responsiveness improvements

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 11 â€” TESTING INFRASTRUCTURE
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Reliable and maintainable system.

Tasks

Unit tests
Integration tests
Service testing
Data layer testing

-------------------------------------------------
PHASE 12 â€” INSTALLER & DEPLOYMENT
-------------------------------------------------

Goals

Smooth installation for customers.

Features

Stable installer package
Dependency verification
Clean publish output
Zero installation errors

-------------------------------------------------
FINAL PRODUCT VISION
-------------------------------------------------

A high-performance offline enterprise desktop system
with modern UI, powerful reporting, reminder system,
leave management, backup infrastructure and
professional user management.

## ARCHITECTURE

# Architecture Baseline

Architecture Style:
Clean Architecture + MVVM

Layers

Domain
Application
Infrastructure
Persistence
Reporting
WPF

Dependency Rules

Domain -> none
Application -> Domain
Infrastructure -> Domain, Application
Persistence -> Domain, Application
Reporting -> Domain, Application
WPF -> all layers

Forbidden

Domain must not reference other layers
Application must not reference WPF

FILE: PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md
=====================================================

# PROJECT DOCUMENTATION

## Project Name
HR and Attendance Management System

## Technology Stack
- .NET 8
- WPF
- Clean Architecture
- Entity Framework Core
- SQLite
- CommunityToolkit.Mvvm
- Serilog
- ClosedXML
- QuestPDF
- LiveCharts
- OpenXML

## Main Modules
- Personnel Management
- Attendance Tracking
- User and Role Management
- Dashboard
- Reports (Excel, PDF, PowerPoint, Charts)
- Backup and Restore
- Persian Reminder System
- Theme / Language / Font Customization

## Architecture Layers
- Domain
- Application
- Infrastructure
- Persistence
- Reporting
- WPF UI

## Current Status
Initial enterprise-grade project skeleton has been created.



=====================================================
FILE: PROJECT_FEATURES.md
=====================================================

# MyCompanyApp.Wpf - Product Vision

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:43

--------------------------------------------------
GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fully Offline Desktop Application
- No internet dependency after deployment
- Enterprise-grade stability
- Zero installation errors policy

--------------------------------------------------
USER INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Modern attractive UI
- Smooth animations
- Custom designed message windows
- Replace default MessageBox with custom dialog system
- Theme switching (Light / Dark / Custom Themes)
- Font switching capability
- Centralized UI styling engine
- Consistent visual identity

--------------------------------------------------
PERSIAN CALENDAR SUPPORT
--------------------------------------------------

- Full Jalali (Shamsi) date support
- Persian date picker
- Internal conversion utilities (Miladi <-> Shamsi)
- Database storage in standard format
- UI display in Persian format

--------------------------------------------------
REMINDER SYSTEM (Similar to Badsaba)
--------------------------------------------------

- Time-based reminders
- Background scheduler
- Notification popup system
- Snooze capability
- Recurring reminders
- Local storage (offline)

--------------------------------------------------
LOGGING SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Application logging
- Database logging
- Error logging
- Performance logging
- Log rotation
- Structured logging format
- Logs stored locally
- Optional database log storage

--------------------------------------------------
BACKUP SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Manual backup
- Automatic scheduled backup
- Database backup
- Restore mechanism
- Backup compression
- Backup versioning

--------------------------------------------------
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fast startup
- Optimized database queries
- Index optimization
- Async operations where required
- Memory usage control
- No UI freezing

--------------------------------------------------
DATABASE OPTIMIZATION
--------------------------------------------------

- Index strategy
- Query performance review
- Profiling support
- Maintenance routines

--------------------------------------------------
TESTABILITY
--------------------------------------------------

- Application layer testable
- Unit tests for core services
- Separated business logic
- No UI-dependent business logic

--------------------------------------------------
INSTALLATION REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Stable installer
- No dependency conflicts
- Clean publish output
- Ready-to-deploy package
- No runtime errors at customer side

--------------------------------------------------
USER MANAGEMENT & ACCESS CONTROL
--------------------------------------------------

- Role-based access control (RBAC)
- User management panel
- Create/Edit/Delete users
- Role definition system
- Permission-based section access
- Feature-level authorization
- Secure authentication
- Password hashing

--------------------------------------------------
FINAL PRODUCT GOAL
--------------------------------------------------

A fully offline, high-performance,
enterprise-grade Persian desktop system
with modern UI and zero instability.

- Theme / Language / Font Customization

## Architecture Layers
- Domain
- Application
- Infrastructure
- Persistence
- Reporting
- WPF UI

## Current Status
Initial enterprise-grade project skeleton has been created.

## PROJECT_FEATURES

# MyCompanyApp.Wpf - Product Vision

Last Updated: 2026-06-14 21:43

--------------------------------------------------
GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fully Offline Desktop Application
- No internet dependency after deployment
- Enterprise-grade stability
- Zero installation errors policy

--------------------------------------------------
USER INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Modern attractive UI
- Smooth animations
- Custom designed message windows
- Replace default MessageBox with custom dialog system
- Theme switching (Light / Dark / Custom Themes)
- Font switching capability
- Centralized UI styling engine
- Consistent visual identity

--------------------------------------------------
PERSIAN CALENDAR SUPPORT
--------------------------------------------------

- Full Jalali (Shamsi) date support
- Persian date picker
- Internal conversion utilities (Miladi <-> Shamsi)
- Database storage in standard format
- UI display in Persian format

--------------------------------------------------
REMINDER SYSTEM (Similar to Badsaba)
--------------------------------------------------

- Time-based reminders
- Background scheduler
- Notification popup system
- Snooze capability
- Recurring reminders
- Local storage (offline)

--------------------------------------------------
LOGGING SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Application logging
- Database logging
- Error logging
- Performance logging
- Log rotation
- Structured logging format
- Logs stored locally
- Optional database log storage

--------------------------------------------------
BACKUP SYSTEM
--------------------------------------------------

- Manual backup
- Automatic scheduled backup
- Database backup
- Restore mechanism
- Backup compression
- Backup versioning

--------------------------------------------------
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Fast startup
- Optimized database queries
- Index optimization
- Async operations where required
- Memory usage control
- No UI freezing

--------------------------------------------------
DATABASE OPTIMIZATION
--------------------------------------------------

- Index strategy
- Query performance review
- Profiling support
- Maintenance routines

--------------------------------------------------
TESTABILITY
--------------------------------------------------

- Application layer testable
- Unit tests for core services
- Separated business logic
- No UI-dependent business logic

--------------------------------------------------
INSTALLATION REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

- Stable installer
- No dependency conflicts
- Clean publish output
- Ready-to-deploy package
- No runtime errors at customer side

--------------------------------------------------
USER MANAGEMENT & ACCESS CONTROL
--------------------------------------------------

- Role-based access control (RBAC)
- User management panel
- Create/Edit/Delete users
- Role definition system
- Permission-based section access
- Feature-level authorization
- Secure authentication
- Password hashing

--------------------------------------------------
FINAL PRODUCT GOAL
--------------------------------------------------

A fully offline, high-performance,
enterprise-grade Persian desktop system
with modern UI and zero instability.

# PROJECT EXECUTION ROADMAP
Generated: 06/15/2026 20:06:42

========================================
PROJECT CORE ARCHITECTURE
========================================

Architecture: Modular Clean Monolith
UI Framework: WPF + MVVM
Database: SQLite
ORM: Entity Framework Core
Security Model: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: EXE
Backup Strategy: Daily Backup (Max 3 Copies)

========================================
IDENTITY & SECURITY RULES
========================================

User Creation:
Only ADMIN can create users.

Authentication:
Username + Password

Password Reset:
- Admin can reset user passwords
- Users can reset their own password

Account Lockout Policy:
- Lock after 3 failed login attempts
- Automatic unlock after 30 minutes
- Admin can manually unlock accounts

Role Model:
- Each user has ONLY ONE role
- Roles are predefined but editable by Admin
- Admin can create / edit / delete roles

Permissions:
- Granular Permission System
- Stored in database
- Assigned to roles

========================================
CORE SYSTEM MODULES
========================================

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Dashboard
Reports
Backup
ImportExport
Settings
Logs
Notifications

All modules are REQUIRED.

========================================
CORE DOMAIN ENTITIES
========================================

Employee
Department
Task
Project
LeaveRequest
Attendance
Notification
ReportDefinition
AuditLog
Setting
BackupRecord
FileAttachment
CalendarEvent
ImportJob
ExportJob

========================================
DEVELOPMENT PHASES
========================================

==================================================

# PROJECT MASTER ROADMAP

This file is the Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Architecture Style: Modular Clean Monolith
Database: SQLite (WAL Enabled)
Security: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: Inno Setup

---

## 17. Implementation Strategy (Execution Freeze)

### 17.1 Implementation Priority (Sequencing)
1. **Database** (Infrastructure/Entities/Persistence)
2. **Security/Auth** (Login/RBAC/Lockout)
3. **Shell/Navigation** (UI Framework/Theme/Navigation)
4. **Dashboard**

### 17.2 Database & Encryption
- Start with stable SQLite.
- After stable migrations, implement SQLCipher.

### 17.3 Startup Flow
1. Splash Screen
2. Backup Check (Automatic)
3. Login
4. Dashboard

### 17.4 Administrative Flow
- First Run: Setup Wizard
- Admin password defined by user

### 17.5 Backup & Restore
- Automatic: Splash + On Exit
- Manual: Admin only
- Restore requires full restart

### 17.6 Module Development Order
1. Settings
2. Departments
3. Employees

### 17.7 Tech Stack Freeze
- UI: WPF + MaterialDesignInXaml
- Validation: FluentValidation
- Mapping: Mapster
- Repository: Aggregates only
- Audit Logs: CRUD + Security events
- Error Handling: Dialog (Critical) + Toast (Info)


==================================================

### 17.8 SSOT Clarification Decisions

These decisions clarify architecture and implementation rules and become binding SSOT rules.

---

DECISION 012 - First Run Bootstrap

On first launch, if no users exist, the application MUST start the Setup Wizard.
The Setup Wizard creates the first ADMIN account.
Username and password are defined by the installer user.
After the first ADMIN exists, the wizard MUST NOT run again.

---

DECISION 013 - Security and RBAC Clarifications

The system initializes with four base roles:

ADMIN
MANAGER
OPERATOR
VIEWER

ADMIN has full system access.
MANAGER has operational control over business modules.
OPERATOR can perform daily operational tasks.
VIEWER has read-only access.

Roles and permissions are stored in the database and editable by ADMIN.

---

DECISION 014 - Dynamic Field Model

Entities supporting dynamic fields in V1:

Customer
Employee
Document
OrganizationProfile

Dynamic fields are stored in metadata tables and rendered dynamically in UI forms.

Each dynamic field supports:

DataType
ValidationRules
IsRequired
IsVisible
DisplayOrder

---

DECISION 015 - Dynamic Reporting Runtime

Reports are metadata-driven.

ReportDefinition defines:

Entity source
Columns
Filters
Sort rules

Execution pipeline:

Metadata -> Query Builder -> EF Core Expression -> Result Grid

Raw SQL execution is not allowed in V1 reporting runtime.

---

DECISION 016 - Extensibility Implementation Rules

The architecture is modular but NOT plugin-based in V1.

Modules are compiled into the main application.

Future plugin architecture may support:

External module loading
Versioned extensions
Module registration

Plugin architecture is deferred until post-V1.

---

DECISION 017 - Backup and Restore Policy

Automatic backup triggers:

Application startup
Application exit

Manual backup:

ADMIN only

Backup includes:

SQLite database
Attachments folder
Critical configuration files

Restore requires full application restart.

Maximum backup copies retained: 3

---

DECISION 018 - Mandatory Modules for V1

The following modules are REQUIRED for version 1:

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Settings
Employees
Departments
Reports
Backup Manager
Logs
Dashboard

The application cannot be considered V1-complete without these modules.

---

DECISION 019 - Deferred Items After V1

The following features are postponed after initial release:

Plugin framework
Multi-organization support
Distributed sync
Advanced analytics engine
Microservice split

---

DECISION 020 - SSOT Precedence Rule

If documentation conflicts exist, priority order is:

1. PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md
2. PROJECT_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
3. PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md
4. PROJECT_IMPLEMENTATION_AUDIT.md

PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md is the final authority.

---

# AI_MASTER_PROMPT

This file is the permanent AI instruction file for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Every AI session must start by reading the daily execution file:

Docs\DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

Then continue based on project documentation and current build status.

The AI must always:
- analyze before coding
- ask clarification questions when needed
- update documentation automatically
- generate one complete PowerShell script
- run restore and build inside scripts
- preserve clean architecture
- prioritize stability and maintainability
