## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Decision Log

Decision-001:
Security system will be implemented ALL FEATURES but PHASED.

Phases:
1. Login + Password Hashing
2. Role Management
3. Permission System (RBAC)
4. Account Lockout
5. Admin Password Reset

Decision-002:
Documentation must be updated after every architectural clarification.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Architecture Decisions

Decision-003:
Roles are ADMIN DEFINED.
Only administrators can create, edit or delete roles.

Decision-004:
Each user can have ONLY ONE role.

Database relation:
User -> RoleId

Decision-005:
Permission model is GRANULAR.

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete

Task.Create
Task.Assign
Task.Complete

Permissions are assigned to Roles.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Decisions Continued

Decision-006:
Users have NO default role.
Admin must select a role during user creation.

Decision-007:
System does NOT include a SuperAdmin role.

Decision-008:
Permissions are stored in the DATABASE.

Decision-009:
Account lockout occurs after 5 failed login attempts.

Unlock policy:
Admin unlock only.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Settings System Decisions

Decision-010:
Settings have two levels.

Global Settings
User Settings

Decision-011:
Settings are stored in DATABASE.

Decision-012:
Settings are categorized.

Categories examples:
General
UI
Security
Backup
System

Decision-013:
Application includes Restore Default Settings feature.

Decision-014:
Every setting must include a DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

`	ext

## Security Implementation Strategy

Security will be implemented using a phased RBAC model.

Core Components:
- User
- Role
- Permission

Security Features:
- Internal Login
- Password Hashing
- Role-Based Access Control
- Account Lockout
- Admin Password Reset


## RBAC Security Model

The application uses Role-Based Access Control.

Model:

User
  -> Role

Role
  -> Permissions

Permission naming convention:

Entity.Action

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete
Task.Assign
Report.View

Constraints:

- Each user has exactly ONE role
- Roles are created and managed by Admin
- Permissions are attached to roles


## Account Lockout Policy

Failed login attempts allowed: 5

After 5 failed attempts:
User account becomes LOCKED.

Unlock strategy:
Admin manual unlock only.

No automatic unlock.


## Settings System Architecture

Levels:

Global Settings
User Settings

Storage:
Database

Each setting contains:

Key
Value
DefaultValue
Category
Scope
Description

Hierarchy:

Default
 -> Global
 -> User

Users may override global settings.

Restore Default feature resets values to DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md

`	ext
# Final Architecture Audit Decisions

## Excel Import/Export
The system shall support Excel import and export. Excel import shall use a Smart Merge strategy with preview and validation before committing data. The system shall report new, updated, duplicated, and invalid records before final import.

## Password Policy
Passwords shall have a medium-strength policy suitable for an offline desktop system.

Rules:
Minimum length: 8 characters  
At least one letter  
At least one digit  
Special characters are optional  

## Installer
The production release shall use an EXE installer. The architecture shall remain compatible with a future MSI installer if enterprise deployment is required.

## Error Handling
The application shall show user-friendly Persian error messages and store full technical error details in both file logs and database logs.

## Backup Policy
The application shall create one daily backup during splash/startup initialization if no backup exists for the current Persian day.

Maximum backups kept: 3

Backup file names shall include the Persian date and time.

Example filename:

Backup_1405-03-25_14-30-10.db

## Logging

The system shall support both:

File logging  
Database logging  

Log categories:

Activity  
Security  
Error  
System  
Backup  
ImportExport  

## Dashboard

The dashboard shall display:

Quick statistics  
Live charts  
Notifications  
System health  
Backup status  

Recent activity shall NOT be displayed on the dashboard, although all activities shall still be logged.

## Notifications

The application shall support:

Toast notifications for immediate feedback  
Notification Center for persistent important notifications

## Modular Structure

The application architecture shall remain a Modular Clean Monolith.

Core modules:

Dashboard  
Identity  
Roles  
Permissions  
Settings  
Reports  
Logs  
Backup  
Notifications  
ImportExport  



``n---

## Source: DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

`	ext
# DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE

Project: MyCompanyApp.Wpf
Project Root: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

## Your Role

You are a senior software architect, WPF engineer, database designer, and AI-driven development assistant.

Your responsibility is to continue this project safely, professionally, and incrementally.

The user must only execute complete PowerShell scripts.
Never ask the user to manually edit project files.

## Mandatory First Step

Before answering or generating development code, you MUST ask the user to provide the latest contents or generated report from:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

If file contents are not available in the chat, generate a PowerShell script that reads these files and prints or exports their content for the user to send back.

## Daily Workflow

### Step 1 - Read Project Knowledge

Analyze the following documentation:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

### Step 2 - Analyze Current State

Detect and report:

- architectural weaknesses
- technical debt
- missing modules
- missing infrastructure
- unclear requirements
- database risks
- SQLite multi-user risks
- security risks
- build risks
- installer and deployment risks
- UI/UX inconsistencies
- documentation inconsistencies

### Step 3 - Ask Clarification Questions

Before continuing development, ask the user important clarification questions when needed.

Questions should focus on:

- database schema design
- entity relationships
- domain rules
- personnel workflows
- task workflows
- leave calculation edge cases
- Persian calendar behavior
- 30-day calendar calculation behavior
- reminder engine behavior
- notification rules
- advanced logging strategy
- backup safety
- role and permission rules
- report and export design
- installer reliability

Do not continue coding if critical design decisions are still unclear.

### Step 4 - Update Documentation Automatically

Whenever the user answers clarification questions, update these files automatically through a complete PowerShell script:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md

Never wait for the user to remind you to update documentation.

### Step 5 - Development Rules

All development output must be ONE complete PowerShell script.

The script must:

- create folders if needed
- create or update files
- create or update C# code
- update documentation when needed
- run dotnet restore
- run dotnet build
- stop on errors
- avoid partial snippets

The user should only copy and paste one PowerShell script.

### Step 6 - Architectural Rules

Maintain this architecture:

- Platform: reusable common infrastructure
- Modules: feature modules
- Apps: WPF application

Keep dependency direction clean.

UI must not contain business logic.
Domain must not depend on WPF.
Infrastructure must not leak into UI.

### Step 7 - Data Access Decision

Use Entity Framework Core with SQLite.

Rules:

- Use EF Core SQLite provider
- Hide DbContext behind repositories/application services where appropriate
- Use UnitOfWork pattern where useful
- Use EF Core Change Tracking or Interceptors for advanced audit logging
- Enable safe SQLite settings for multi-user usage:
  - WAL mode
  - busy timeout
  - short transactions
  - retry handling where possible
- Avoid long-running write transactions

### Step 8 - Product Goal

Build a stable offline multi-user WPF desktop platform in Persian with:

- personnel management
- editable departments
- editable personnel statuses
- authentication and authorization
- checkbox-based permission management
- task management with steps
- multi-user task assignment
- task dependencies
- leave management
- Persian calendar
- normal and 30-day calculation mode per leave/reminder record
- official holidays
- admin-defined holidays
- reminder engine
- snooze/repeat/windows notification settings per reminder
- advanced audit logging
- reports
- Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export
- backup and restore
- simple manual update model
- professional Windows 11 style UI
- friendly Persian messages

### Step 9 - Session Output Format

Every session response must include:

1. Project understanding summary
2. Detected weaknesses and risks
3. Clarification questions, if needed
4. Proposed next safe development step
5. One complete PowerShell script, only when enough information exists
6. Reminder of what file/report the user should send next time

Wait for user confirmation before moving to the next phase.


``n---

# Architecture Decisions ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ 2026-06-16

These decisions were confirmed during architecture clarification.

## Decision 001 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Database Naming Convention
Chosen Option: **d**

## Decision 002 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Domain Entity Strategy
Chosen Option: **b**

## Decision 003 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Audit Logging Strategy
Chosen Model: **Hybrid Audit**

Meaning:
- Critical actions logged in database
- Technical logs stored in file logs
- Security events duplicated for safety

## Decision 004 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Task Status Design

## Decision 005 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reminder System
Architecture: **Generic Reminder Engine**

Capabilities:
- Works with multiple modules
- Supports snooze / repeat
- Supports multiple notification channels

## Decision 006 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Import Architecture
Strategy: **ImportService per module**

Example:
- EmployeeImportService
- DepartmentImportService
- TaskImportService

Advantages:
- Clear validation rules
- Module isolation
- Easier maintenance

## Decision 007 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reporting Filters
Design:
**Dynamic Filter + Predefined Dataset**

Meaning:
- User-defined filters
- Prebuilt datasets for common reports
- Flexible reporting layer

## Decision 008 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Backup Configuration

**BackupLocation Setting**

## Decision 010 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Final Architecture Opinion

Recommended approach:

Hybrid enterprise desktop architecture combining:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- Metadata-driven reporting
- Dynamic field configuration
- Generic reminder engine
- RBAC security model

This ensures:

- maintainability
- scalability
- modular growth
- enterprise stability

## Decision 011 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Selected Option
Chosen Option: **a**

# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## 1. Architectural Intent

The project supports an extensibility model centered on:

- metadata-driven reporting
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven settings
- controlled customization per organization
- maintainable core architecture

The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.

---

## 2. Confirmed Dynamic Components

The roadmap already includes the following extensibility-related building blocks:

- EntityFieldSetting
- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

These components are used to support:

- field activation and visibility
- optional fields
- database-driven report definitions
- configurable report columns
- configurable report filters
- configurable report sorting

---

## 3. Customization Rules

Any customization must follow these rules:

1. **Database-driven first**
   - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.

2. **Core stability**
   - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.

3. **Explicit field control**
   - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.

4. **Report configuration separation**
   - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.

5. **Safe optional-field strategy**
   - Optional entity fields should follow the existing EmployeeOptionalFields approach when a structured extension is needed.

---

## 4. Dynamic Field Strategy

The project should use dynamic fields for cases where:

- a field may be enabled or disabled per deployment
- a field is optional for some organizations
- a field requires visibility control
- a field needs metadata-based behavior

The roadmap already confirms metadata-driven field activation and visibility, so this is the official direction for dynamic field behavior.

---

## 5. Dynamic Reporting Strategy

The reporting layer should remain metadata-driven.

That means reports can be defined using:

- a report definition
- selected columns
- filters
- sorting rules

This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

---

## 6. Field Settings Service

The presence of:

- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService

confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.

This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

---

## 7. Extensibility Boundary

This extensibility model is **not** a plugin framework.

It is a controlled metadata-driven customization approach for:

- field behavior
- report behavior
- optional structured data
- settings-based runtime decisions

No plugin architecture is introduced by this document unless explicitly added later in the SSOT.

---

## 8. Resulting Design Direction

The confirmed design direction is:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- metadata-driven customization
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven reporting metadata
- maintainable and enterprise-stable implementation

This keeps the system flexible while protecting the core codebase.

---

# ENTERPRISE DATABASE BLUEPRINT

## 1. Purpose

This section defines the official enterprise-grade database blueprint for the project.

The database must support:

- Modular Clean Monolith architecture
- WPF desktop deployment
- SQLite as the local relational database engine
- EF Core as the ORM
- RBAC security model
- auditability
- soft delete
- dynamic field configuration
- metadata-driven reporting
- reminder engine
- backup and restore
- maintainable future growth

This blueprint is part of the Single Source of Truth and must be used as the reference for all future entity, DbContext, migration, repository, and service implementations.

---

## 2. Database Design Rules

### 2.1 Common Entity Columns

Most business tables should include the following fields:

| Column | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Id | INTEGER / GUID | Yes | Primary key |
| CreatedAt | DateTime | Yes | Creation timestamp |
| CreatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who created the record |
| UpdatedAt | DateTime | No | Last update timestamp |
| UpdatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who last updated the record |
| IsDeleted | Boolean | Yes | Soft delete flag |
| DeletedAt | DateTime | No | Delete timestamp |
| DeletedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who deleted the record |
| RowVersion | INTEGER / BLOB | No | Concurrency control |

### 2.2 Soft Delete Rule

Business records should not be physically deleted by default.

Instead:

- IsDeleted = true
- DeletedAt is set
- DeletedByUserId is set when available

Hard delete is only allowed for temporary, cache, or system maintenance tables.

### 2.3 Audit Rule

Important business actions must be auditable.

Audit logs should capture:

- who performed the action
- what entity was affected
- what operation was performed
- when it happened
- previous values where practical
- new values where practical

### 2.4 Metadata Rule

Dynamic fields, dynamic reports, and configurable behavior must be database-driven.

Confirmed metadata-driven concepts include:

- EntityFieldSetting
- dynamic field activation
- dynamic field visibility
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

### 2.5 SQLite Compatibility Rule

The schema must remain compatible with SQLite.

Avoid database features that are not portable or are difficult to maintain in SQLite, such as:

- stored procedures
- advanced server-side jobs
- database-specific procedural logic
- complex trigger-heavy behavior unless absolutely necessary

Business rules should primarily live in the application layer.

---

## 3. Table Groups Overview

The database is divided into the following logical groups:

1. Identity and Security
2. Organization and Application Settings
3. People and Business Entities
4. Documents and Attachments
5. Dynamic Fields and Metadata
6. Reporting
7. Reminder and Notification Engine
8. Audit and Activity Tracking
9. Backup and Maintenance
10. Import / Export
11. System Diagnostics

---

## 4. Identity and Security Tables

### 4.1 Users

Stores application users.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Username
- PasswordHash
- PasswordSalt
- DisplayName
- Email
- PhoneNumber
- IsActive
- IsSystemUser
- LastLoginAt
- FailedLoginCount
- LockoutEndAt
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId
- IsDeleted
- DeletedAt
- DeletedByUserId

### 4.2 Roles

## 15. Recommended Initial Implementation Order

Database implementation should be done in this order:

1. Base entity abstractions
2. Users
3. Roles
4. Permissions
5. UserRoles
6. RolePermissions
7. SystemSettings
8. OrganizationProfile
9. LookupCategories
10. LookupItems
11. Customers
12. Employees
13. EmployeeOptionalFields
14. EntityFieldSettings
15. ReportDefinitions
16. ReportColumns
17. ReportFilters
18. ReportSorts
19. AuditLogs
20. ErrorLogs
21. BackupSettings
22. BackupJobs
23. Reminders
24. Notifications

This sequence allows the system to bootstrap security, settings, domain data, metadata, reporting, logging, and backup gradually.

---

## 17. Implementation Notes

This blueprint is intentionally broader than the first development phase.

Not all tables must be implemented immediately.

The first implementation milestone should focus on:

- identity and security
- settings
- customers
- employees
- dynamic fields
- dynamic reports
- audit logs
- error logs
- backup settings

Additional tables should be implemented when their module becomes active.

---

## 18. SSOT Rule

If any future document, note, roadmap, audit, or implementation log conflicts with this database blueprint, this section in PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md takes precedence.

---

# SSOT CLARIFICATION DECISIONS

> This section resolves the remaining architectural and implementation ambiguities in the project roadmap.
> These decisions are part of the official Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.
> If another document conflicts with this section, this section prevails unless explicitly superseded in a later SSOT decision.

---

## DECISION 012 â€” FIRST-RUN BOOTSTRAP AND INITIAL ADMIN

### Status
Accepted.

### Problem
The security rules state that only an ADMIN can create users.  
However, a fresh installation has no existing ADMIN account.

### Decision
The system shall use a First-Run Setup Wizard to create the initial ADMIN account.

### Rules
1. On first application startup, the system checks whether any user exists.
2. If no user exists, the application enters First-Run Setup Mode.
3. First-Run Setup Mode shall require the operator to create the initial ADMIN account.
4. The first account shall be assigned the ADMIN role automatically.
5. The initial ADMIN account shall be marked as the bootstrap administrator.
6. After the first ADMIN is created, First-Run Setup Mode shall be disabled.
7. After bootstrap, new users can only be created by an ADMIN.
8. The first ADMIN password shall be hashed using the same production password hashing policy as all other users.
9. The bootstrap flow shall be auditable.

### Initial Admin Fields
The First-Run Setup Wizard shall collect at minimum:

- Full name
- Username
- Password
- Password confirmation
- Optional email or phone number

### Security Requirements
- Username must be unique.
- Password must satisfy the active password policy.
- Password must never be stored as plain text.
- Bootstrap events must be written to audit logs.
- Re-running bootstrap must be impossible unless the database is empty or explicitly reset by a controlled maintenance process.

### Implementation Direction
The bootstrap check shall be performed through an application service, not directly from the UI.

Recommended service names:

- `IFirstRunSetupService`
- `FirstRunSetupService`
- `IIdentityBootstrapService`
- `IdentityBootstrapService`

---

## DECISION 013 â€” SECURITY AND RBAC CLARIFICATIONS

### Status
Accepted.

### Existing Rule
The project uses database-driven RBAC with granular permissions.

### Clarified RBAC Model
1. Each user has exactly one primary role.
2. Roles are stored in the database.
3. Permissions are stored in the database.
4. Permissions are assigned to roles.
5. ADMIN can manage users, roles, and role-permission assignments.
6. User-specific permission overrides are allowed only when explicitly needed and must be auditable.

### Default Roles
The system shall seed the following default roles:

| Role | Purpose |
|---|---|
| ADMIN | Full system administration |
| MANAGER | Operational management access |
| OPERATOR | Normal daily data entry and processing |
| VIEWER | Read-only access where permitted |

### Default Permission Behavior
- ADMIN receives all permissions.
- Other roles receive minimum required permissions.
- Permission changes must be persisted in the database.
- Permission checks must be performed in the application layer and enforced by UI visibility/enablement where applicable.

### Account Lockout
The following rules remain official:

- Lock account after 3 failed login attempts.
- Auto-unlock after 30 minutes.
- ADMIN can manually unlock accounts.
- Login failures and unlock events must be audited.

### Password Reset
- ADMIN can reset another user's password.
- A user can change their own password.
- Password reset/change events must be audited.

---

## DECISION 014 â€” DYNAMIC FIELD MODEL

### Status
Accepted.

### Problem
The roadmap defines metadata-driven fields, but the exact scope and runtime behavior need clarification.

### Decision
Dynamic fields shall be implemented as controlled metadata-driven extensions for approved entities only.

### Dynamic-Field-Capable Entities for V1
The following entities are dynamic-field-capable in V1:

| Entity | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Customer | Customer-specific custom data |
| Employee | Employee-specific custom data |
| Document | Document classification and custom metadata |
| OrganizationProfile | Organization-specific configurable profile fields |

### Deferred Dynamic-Field Entities
The following may be considered after V1:

- Project
- Contract
- Asset
- Invoice
- Task
- Any future module-specific entity

### Metadata Storage
Dynamic field metadata shall be stored in `EntityFieldSetting` or related field metadata tables.

Recommended metadata properties:

| Property | Description |
|---|---|
| EntityName | Target entity name |
| FieldKey | Stable internal field identifier |
| DisplayName | User-visible field title |
| FieldType | Data type such as Text, Number, Date, Boolean, Lookup |
| IsRequired | Whether value is mandatory |
| IsVisible | Whether field appears in UI |
| IsEditable | Whether user can edit the field |
| MaxLength | Optional maximum text length |
| RegexPattern | Optional validation pattern |
| DefaultValue | Optional default value |
| SortOrder | Display order |
| GroupName | Optional UI grouping |
| HelpText | Optional user guidance |
| IsSystemField | Whether the field is protected from deletion |

### Field Types for V1
The following field types are supported in V1:

- Text
- LongText
- Number
- Decimal
- Date
- DateTime
- Boolean
- Lookup

### Validation Rules
1. Dynamic field validation shall be performed in the application layer.
2. Required fields must be validated before saving.
3. Max length must be enforced for text fields.
4. Regex validation may be used for formatted text values.
5. Lookup values must be validated against the configured lookup source.
6. Invalid dynamic field values must produce user-friendly validation messages.

### UI Rules
1. UI shall read dynamic field metadata from the application service.
2. UI shall not hardcode dynamic fields.
3. Field visibility and editability shall be controlled by metadata and permissions.
4. Dynamic field rendering shall be deterministic and ordered by `SortOrder`.

### Data Storage Strategy
For V1, the preferred approach is:

- Use dedicated optional-field tables for important stable optional data where already defined.
- Use metadata/value tables for truly dynamic fields.

This preserves performance and type safety for known fields while allowing controlled customization.

Recommended services:

- `IEntityFieldSettingsService`
- `EntityFieldSettingsService`
- `IDynamicFieldValidationService`
- `DynamicFieldValidationService`

---

## DECISION 015 â€” DYNAMIC REPORTING RUNTIME

### Status
Accepted.

### Problem
The roadmap defines report metadata entities but does not fully define how reports are executed.

### Decision
Reports shall be executed using metadata-driven query composition in the application layer.

### Core Reporting Entities
The reporting system shall use:

- `ReportDefinition`
- `ReportColumn`
- `ReportFilter`
- `ReportSort`
- `ReportExecution`
- `ReportExportFile`

### Runtime Flow
A report execution shall follow this process:

1. Load `ReportDefinition`.
2. Load active `ReportColumn` records.
3. Load active `ReportFilter` records.
4. Load active `ReportSort` records.
5. Resolve the report source.
6. Build a safe query through an application service.
7. Apply filters.
8. Apply sorting.
9. Project selected columns.
10. Return tabular result.
11. Optionally export to supported formats.

### Query Strategy
The reporting runtime shall use LINQ-based query composition over approved report sources.

Raw SQL report execution is not part of V1 unless explicitly approved later.

### Approved V1 Report Sources
V1 reports may be built over the following sources:

- Customers
- Employees
- Documents
- Audit logs
- Error logs
- Backup jobs
- System settings summary

### V1 Filter Operators
The following filter operators are supported in V1:

| Operator | Description |
|---|---|
| Equal | Exact match |
| NotEqual | Negative exact match |
| Contains | Text contains |
| StartsWith | Text prefix |
| EndsWith | Text suffix |
| GreaterThan | Numeric/date comparison |
| GreaterThanOrEqual | Numeric/date comparison |
| LessThan | Numeric/date comparison |
| LessThanOrEqual | Numeric/date comparison |
| Between | Range comparison |
| IsNull | Null check |
| IsNotNull | Non-null check |

### V1 Sort Rules
- Sort field must be part of an approved source.
- Sort direction must be Ascending or Descending.
- Multiple sort rules are allowed and applied by `SortOrder`.

### Security
1. Report execution must respect user permissions.
2. Users must not access data through reports that they cannot access through normal screens.
3. Report definitions must be manageable only by authorized users.
4. Report execution should be audit-friendly.

### Deferred Reporting Features
The following are deferred after V1:

- Arbitrary SQL reports
- Cross-database reports
- User-authored expression language
- Complex joins designed by end-users
- Scheduled report delivery
- External BI integration

Recommended services:

- `IReportExecutionService`
- `ReportExecutionService`
- `IReportQueryBuilder`
- `ReportQueryBuilder`
- `IReportExportService`
- `ReportExportService`

---

## DECISION 016 â€” EXTENSIBILITY IMPLEMENTATION RULES

### Status
Accepted.

### Existing Principle
The system is an Extensible Modular Platform.

### Clarification
The platform is extensible through modular architecture, metadata, configuration, and database-driven definitions.

It is not a general-purpose third-party plugin framework in V1.

### Supported Extension Types
The system may support the following extension types:

| Extension Type | V1 Support |
|---|---|
| Add new field to approved entity | Supported |
| Configure field visibility/editability | Supported |
| Add metadata-driven report | Supported |
| Add lookup categories/items | Supported |
| Add new internal module by developer | Supported |
| Add new table through developer-managed migration | Supported |
| Add arbitrary external plugin DLL | Deferred |
| Runtime loading of third-party modules | Deferred |

### Module Registration
Internal modules shall be registered explicitly in application startup or module registration classes.

Recommended concepts:

- `IModuleDefinition`
- `ModuleDefinition`
- `IModuleRegistry`
- `ModuleRegistry`

### Module Metadata
A module definition should include:

- Module key
- Display name
- Description
- Version
- Required permissions
- Navigation entries
- Service registrations
- Optional settings pages
- Optional report definitions

### Migration Policy
1. Schema changes must be handled through EF Core migrations.
2. Dynamic metadata changes must be stored in database tables.
3. V1 does not support automatic execution of unknown third-party migrations.
4. Developer-created modules may add migrations as part of normal application releases.

### Versioning
Each internal module should expose a module version for diagnostics and future upgrade paths.

### Explicit Non-Goals for V1
The following are not part of V1:

- Third-party plugin marketplace
- Hot-loading external assemblies
- Sandboxed extension runtime
- User-authored code execution
- Automatic third-party database migrations

---

## DECISION 017 â€” BACKUP AND RESTORE POLICY

### Status
Accepted.

### Existing Rule
The system uses daily backup with a maximum of 3 copies.

### Clarified Backup Scope
A complete application backup shall include:

1. SQLite database file.
2. Required attachment/document files.
3. Critical local configuration files required for restore.
4. Backup metadata.

### Backup Frequency
- Automatic backup shall run daily.
- Manual backup shall be available to ADMIN users.
- Backup may also run on safe application exit where practical.

### Backup Retention
- Maximum retained automatic backups: 3.
- When more than 3 automatic backups exist, the oldest backup shall be deleted.
- Manual backups may be kept separately if explicitly marked as manual.

### Backup Location
- Backup location is controlled by `BackupLocation`.
- `BackupLocation` is stored in the system settings table.
- If no backup location is configured, the application shall use a safe default local application data path.

### Backup Format
A backup package should contain:

- Database copy
- Attachments folder snapshot or archive
- Metadata file containing:
  - backup timestamp
  - application version
  - database version
  - backup type
  - machine/user context where applicable

### Restore Policy
1. Restore is ADMIN-only.
2. Restore requires explicit user confirmation.
3. Restore must create a pre-restore safety backup before replacing active data.
4. Restore requires full application restart.
5. Restore events must be audited.
6. Restore must validate backup integrity before applying it.

### Encryption
Backup encryption is recommended but deferred unless explicitly required by deployment policy.

### Deferred Backup Features
The following are deferred after V1:

- Cloud backup
- Remote backup server
- Encrypted backup by default
- Differential backup
- Point-in-time restore

Recommended services:

- `IDatabaseBackupService`
- `DatabaseBackupService`
- `IApplicationBackupService`
- `ApplicationBackupService`
- `IRestoreService`
- `RestoreService`
- `IBackupRetentionService`
- `BackupRetentionService`

---

## DECISION 018 â€” V1 MANDATORY MODULE SCOPE

### Status
Accepted.

### Problem
The roadmap contains a broad enterprise blueprint, but V1 implementation must remain achievable.

### Decision
V1 shall focus on the mandatory foundation modules required to make the system stable, installable, secure, and extensible.

### Mandatory V1 Modules
The following modules are mandatory for V1:

| Module | Reason |
|---|---|
| Identity and Security | Required for all protected operations |
| Settings | Required for configurable behavior |
| Organization Profile | Required for single-organization deployment |
| Customers | Core business entity |
| Employees | Core business entity |
| Dynamic Fields | Required for extensibility |
| Dynamic Reporting | Required for metadata-driven reporting |
| Audit Logging | Required for accountability |
| Error Logging | Required for diagnostics |
| Backup | Required by roadmap |
| Restore | Required to complete backup lifecycle |
| Dashboard | Required for operational entry point |
| Installer/Deployment | Required for customer installation |

### Mandatory V1 Database Tables
The V1 implementation should prioritize these tables:

- Users
- Roles
- Permissions
- UserRoles
- RolePermissions
- UserPermissionOverrides
- LoginHistory
- SystemSettings
- OrganizationProfile
- Customers
- Employees
- EmployeeOptionalFields
- EntityFieldSettings
- EntityFieldDefinitions
- EntityFieldValues
- ReportDefinitions
- ReportColumns
- ReportFilters
- ReportSorts
- ReportExecutions
- AuditLogs
- ErrorLogs
- BackupSettings
- BackupJobs
- RestoreHistory
- AppVersions
- DatabaseMigrations

### V1 Completion Criteria
V1 is considered complete only when:

1. Application installs and runs through EXE installer.
2. First-run setup creates the initial ADMIN.
3. ADMIN can manage users, roles, and permissions.
4. Login, lockout, password reset, and logout work.
5. Core settings can be configured.
6. Customers and Employees can be managed.
7. Dynamic fields work for approved entities.
8. Dynamic reports can be defined and executed for approved sources.
9. Audit and error logs are persisted.
10. Daily backup works and retains max 3 copies.
11. Restore workflow exists and requires restart.
12. Build and deployment package are reproducible.

---

## DECISION 019 â€” DEFERRED ITEMS AFTER V1

### Status
Accepted.

### Purpose
This decision prevents V1 scope creep.

### Deferred After V1
The following items are intentionally deferred unless explicitly promoted into V1 by a later SSOT decision:

- Third-party plugin framework
- External DLL hot-loading
- Plugin marketplace
- Complex user-designed joins in reporting
- Arbitrary SQL reporting
- Cloud backup
- Remote backup server
- Encrypted backup by default
- Multi-organization / multi-tenant deployment
- Web version
- Mobile application
- External identity providers
- Full workflow engine
- Advanced notification scheduling
- BI integration
- User-authored scripts
- Sandboxed custom code execution

### Rule
Deferred items must not block V1 implementation.

---

## DECISION 020 â€” SSOT PRECEDENCE RULE

### Status
Accepted.

### Decision
`PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md` is the official Single Source of Truth.

### Precedence
If project documents conflict, the precedence order is:

1. `PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md`
2. Explicit implementation audit findings
3. Execution roadmap documents
4. Aggregated documentation files
5. Generated or historical notes

### Documentation Rule
Other documentation files may summarize or explain, but they must not override the master roadmap.

### Change Rule
Any future architectural change must be recorded in `PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md`.

---

# Architecture Baseline

Architecture Style:
Clean Architecture + MVVM

Layers

Domain
Application
Infrastructure
Persistence
Reporting
WPF

Dependency Rules

Domain -> none
Application -> Domain
Infrastructure -> Domain, Application
Persistence -> Domain, Application
Reporting -> Domain, Application
WPF -> all layers

Forbidden

Domain must not reference other layers
Application must not reference WPF

# PROJECT EXECUTION ROADMAP
Generated: 06/15/2026 20:06:42

========================================
PROJECT CORE ARCHITECTURE
========================================

Architecture: Modular Clean Monolith
UI Framework: WPF + MVVM
Database: SQLite
ORM: Entity Framework Core
Security Model: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: EXE
Backup Strategy: Daily Backup (Max 3 Copies)

========================================
IDENTITY & SECURITY RULES
========================================

User Creation:
Only ADMIN can create users.

Authentication:
Username + Password

Password Reset:
- Admin can reset user passwords
- Users can reset their own password

Account Lockout Policy:
- Lock after 3 failed login attempts
- Automatic unlock after 30 minutes
- Admin can manually unlock accounts

Role Model:
- Each user has ONLY ONE role
- Roles are predefined but editable by Admin
- Admin can create / edit / delete roles

Permissions:
- Granular Permission System
- Stored in database
- Assigned to roles

========================================
CORE SYSTEM MODULES
========================================

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Dashboard
Reports
Backup
ImportExport
Settings
Logs
Notifications

All modules are REQUIRED.

========================================
CORE DOMAIN ENTITIES
========================================

Employee
Department
Task
Project
LeaveRequest
Attendance
Notification
ReportDefinition
AuditLog
Setting
BackupRecord
FileAttachment
CalendarEvent
ImportJob
ExportJob

==================================================

# PROJECT MASTER ROADMAP

This file is the Single Source of Truth for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Architecture Style: Modular Clean Monolith
Database: SQLite (WAL Enabled)
Security: RBAC
Logging: File + Database
Installer: Inno Setup

---

## 17. Implementation Strategy (Execution Freeze)

### 17.1 Implementation Priority (Sequencing)
1. **Database** (Infrastructure/Entities/Persistence)
2. **Security/Auth** (Login/RBAC/Lockout)
3. **Shell/Navigation** (UI Framework/Theme/Navigation)
4. **Dashboard**

### 17.2 Database & Encryption
- Start with stable SQLite.
- After stable migrations, implement SQLCipher.

### 17.3 Startup Flow
1. Splash Screen
2. Backup Check (Automatic)
3. Login
4. Dashboard

### 17.4 Administrative Flow
- First Run: Setup Wizard
- Admin password defined by user

### 17.5 Backup & Restore
- Automatic: Splash + On Exit
- Manual: Admin only
- Restore requires full restart

### 17.6 Module Development Order
1. Settings
2. Departments
3. Employees

### 17.7 Tech Stack Freeze
- UI: WPF + MaterialDesignInXaml
- Validation: FluentValidation
- Mapping: Mapster
- Repository: Aggregates only
- Audit Logs: CRUD + Security events
- Error Handling: Dialog (Critical) + Toast (Info)


==================================================

### 17.8 SSOT Clarification Decisions

These decisions clarify architecture and implementation rules and become binding SSOT rules.

---

DECISION 012 - First Run Bootstrap

On first launch, if no users exist, the application MUST start the Setup Wizard.
The Setup Wizard creates the first ADMIN account.
Username and password are defined by the installer user.
After the first ADMIN exists, the wizard MUST NOT run again.

---

DECISION 013 - Security and RBAC Clarifications

The system initializes with four base roles:

ADMIN
MANAGER
OPERATOR
VIEWER

ADMIN has full system access.
MANAGER has operational control over business modules.
OPERATOR can perform daily operational tasks.
VIEWER has read-only access.

Roles and permissions are stored in the database and editable by ADMIN.

---

DECISION 014 - Dynamic Field Model

Entities supporting dynamic fields in V1:

Customer
Employee
Document
OrganizationProfile

Dynamic fields are stored in metadata tables and rendered dynamically in UI forms.

Each dynamic field supports:

DataType
ValidationRules
IsRequired
IsVisible
DisplayOrder

---

DECISION 015 - Dynamic Reporting Runtime

Reports are metadata-driven.

ReportDefinition defines:

Entity source
Columns
Filters
Sort rules

Execution pipeline:

Metadata -> Query Builder -> EF Core Expression -> Result Grid

Raw SQL execution is not allowed in V1 reporting runtime.

---

DECISION 016 - Extensibility Implementation Rules

The architecture is modular but NOT plugin-based in V1.

Modules are compiled into the main application.

Future plugin architecture may support:

External module loading
Versioned extensions
Module registration

Plugin architecture is deferred until post-V1.

---

DECISION 017 - Backup and Restore Policy

Automatic backup triggers:

Application startup
Application exit

Manual backup:

ADMIN only

Backup includes:

SQLite database
Attachments folder
Critical configuration files

Restore requires full application restart.

Maximum backup copies retained: 3

---

DECISION 018 - Mandatory Modules for V1

The following modules are REQUIRED for version 1:

Identity
Roles
Permissions
Settings
Employees
Departments
Reports
Backup Manager
Logs
Dashboard

The application cannot be considered V1-complete without these modules.

---

DECISION 019 - Deferred Items After V1

The following features are postponed after initial release:

Plugin framework
Multi-organization support
Distributed sync
Advanced analytics engine
Microservice split

---

DECISION 020 - SSOT Precedence Rule

If documentation conflicts exist, priority order is:

1. PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md
2. PROJECT_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
3. PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md
4. PROJECT_IMPLEMENTATION_AUDIT.md

PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md is the final authority.

---

## Source: AI_MASTER_PROMPT.txt

`	ext
# AI_MASTER_PROMPT

This file is the permanent AI instruction file for MyCompanyApp.Wpf.

Every AI session must start by reading the daily execution file:

Docs\DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

Then continue based on project documentation and current build status.

The AI must always:
- analyze before coding
- ask clarification questions when needed
- update documentation automatically
- generate one complete PowerShell script
- run restore and build inside scripts
- preserve clean architecture
- prioritize stability and maintainability


``n---

## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Decision Log

Decision-001:
Security system will be implemented ALL FEATURES but PHASED.

Phases:
1. Login + Password Hashing
2. Role Management
3. Permission System (RBAC)
4. Account Lockout
5. Admin Password Reset

Decision-002:
Documentation must be updated after every architectural clarification.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Architecture Decisions

Decision-003:
Roles are ADMIN DEFINED.
Only administrators can create, edit or delete roles.

Decision-004:
Each user can have ONLY ONE role.

Database relation:
User -> RoleId

Decision-005:
Permission model is GRANULAR.

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete

Task.Create
Task.Assign
Task.Complete

Permissions are assigned to Roles.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Security Decisions Continued

Decision-006:
Users have NO default role.
Admin must select a role during user creation.

Decision-007:
System does NOT include a SuperAdmin role.

Decision-008:
Permissions are stored in the DATABASE.

Decision-009:
Account lockout occurs after 5 failed login attempts.

Unlock policy:
Admin unlock only.


## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Settings System Decisions

Decision-010:
Settings have two levels.

Global Settings
User Settings

Decision-011:
Settings are stored in DATABASE.

Decision-012:
Settings are categorized.

Categories examples:
General
UI
Security
Backup
System

Decision-013:
Application includes Restore Default Settings feature.

Decision-014:
Every setting must include a DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

`	ext

## Security Implementation Strategy

Security will be implemented using a phased RBAC model.

Core Components:
- User
- Role
- Permission

Security Features:
- Internal Login
- Password Hashing
- Role-Based Access Control
- Account Lockout
- Admin Password Reset


## RBAC Security Model

The application uses Role-Based Access Control.

Model:

User
  -> Role

Role
  -> Permissions

Permission naming convention:

Entity.Action

Examples:
User.Create
User.Edit
User.Delete
Task.Assign
Report.View

Constraints:

- Each user has exactly ONE role
- Roles are created and managed by Admin
- Permissions are attached to roles


## Account Lockout Policy

Failed login attempts allowed: 5

After 5 failed attempts:
User account becomes LOCKED.

Unlock strategy:
Admin manual unlock only.

No automatic unlock.


## Settings System Architecture

Levels:

Global Settings
User Settings

Storage:
Database

Each setting contains:

Key
Value
DefaultValue
Category
Scope
Description

Hierarchy:

Default
 -> Global
 -> User

Users may override global settings.

Restore Default feature resets values to DefaultValue.



``n---

## Source: ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md

`	ext
# Final Architecture Audit Decisions

## Excel Import/Export
The system shall support Excel import and export. Excel import shall use a Smart Merge strategy with preview and validation before committing data. The system shall report new, updated, duplicated, and invalid records before final import.

## Password Policy
Passwords shall have a medium-strength policy suitable for an offline desktop system.

Rules:
Minimum length: 8 characters  
At least one letter  
At least one digit  
Special characters are optional  

## Installer
The production release shall use an EXE installer. The architecture shall remain compatible with a future MSI installer if enterprise deployment is required.

## Error Handling
The application shall show user-friendly Persian error messages and store full technical error details in both file logs and database logs.

## Backup Policy
The application shall create one daily backup during splash/startup initialization if no backup exists for the current Persian day.

Maximum backups kept: 3

Backup file names shall include the Persian date and time.

Example filename:

Backup_1405-03-25_14-30-10.db

## Logging

The system shall support both:

File logging  
Database logging  

Log categories:

Activity  
Security  
Error  
System  
Backup  
ImportExport  

## Dashboard

The dashboard shall display:

Quick statistics  
Live charts  
Notifications  
System health  
Backup status  

Recent activity shall NOT be displayed on the dashboard, although all activities shall still be logged.

## Notifications

The application shall support:

Toast notifications for immediate feedback  
Notification Center for persistent important notifications

## Modular Structure

The application architecture shall remain a Modular Clean Monolith.

Core modules:

Dashboard  
Identity  
Roles  
Permissions  
Settings  
Reports  
Logs  
Backup  
Notifications  
ImportExport  



``n---

## Source: DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE.txt

`	ext
# DAILY_AI_EXECUTION_FILE

Project: MyCompanyApp.Wpf
Project Root: G:\Projects\Computer\MyProjects\MyCompanyApp.Wpf

## Your Role

You are a senior software architect, WPF engineer, database designer, and AI-driven development assistant.

Your responsibility is to continue this project safely, professionally, and incrementally.

The user must only execute complete PowerShell scripts.
Never ask the user to manually edit project files.

## Mandatory First Step

Before answering or generating development code, you MUST ask the user to provide the latest contents or generated report from:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

If file contents are not available in the chat, generate a PowerShell script that reads these files and prints or exports their content for the user to send back.

## Daily Workflow

### Step 1 - Read Project Knowledge

Analyze the following documentation:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
- Docs\AI_AUTOMATED_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md, if available

### Step 2 - Analyze Current State

Detect and report:

- architectural weaknesses
- technical debt
- missing modules
- missing infrastructure
- unclear requirements
- database risks
- SQLite multi-user risks
- security risks
- build risks
- installer and deployment risks
- UI/UX inconsistencies
- documentation inconsistencies

### Step 3 - Ask Clarification Questions

Before continuing development, ask the user important clarification questions when needed.

Questions should focus on:

- database schema design
- entity relationships
- domain rules
- personnel workflows
- task workflows
- leave calculation edge cases
- Persian calendar behavior
- 30-day calendar calculation behavior
- reminder engine behavior
- notification rules
- advanced logging strategy
- backup safety
- role and permission rules
- report and export design
- installer reliability

Do not continue coding if critical design decisions are still unclear.

### Step 4 - Update Documentation Automatically

Whenever the user answers clarification questions, update these files automatically through a complete PowerShell script:

- Docs\AI_PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
- Docs\PROJECT_FEATURES.md
- Docs\AI_DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md
- Docs\AI_TASK_LOG.md
- Docs\ARCHITECTURE.md

Never wait for the user to remind you to update documentation.

### Step 5 - Development Rules

All development output must be ONE complete PowerShell script.

The script must:

- create folders if needed
- create or update files
- create or update C# code
- update documentation when needed
- run dotnet restore
- run dotnet build
- stop on errors
- avoid partial snippets

The user should only copy and paste one PowerShell script.

### Step 6 - Architectural Rules

Maintain this architecture:

- Platform: reusable common infrastructure
- Modules: feature modules
- Apps: WPF application

Keep dependency direction clean.

UI must not contain business logic.
Domain must not depend on WPF.
Infrastructure must not leak into UI.

### Step 7 - Data Access Decision

Use Entity Framework Core with SQLite.

Rules:

- Use EF Core SQLite provider
- Hide DbContext behind repositories/application services where appropriate
- Use UnitOfWork pattern where useful
- Use EF Core Change Tracking or Interceptors for advanced audit logging
- Enable safe SQLite settings for multi-user usage:
  - WAL mode
  - busy timeout
  - short transactions
  - retry handling where possible
- Avoid long-running write transactions

### Step 8 - Product Goal

Build a stable offline multi-user WPF desktop platform in Persian with:

- personnel management
- editable departments
- editable personnel statuses
- authentication and authorization
- checkbox-based permission management
- task management with steps
- multi-user task assignment
- task dependencies
- leave management
- Persian calendar
- normal and 30-day calculation mode per leave/reminder record
- official holidays
- admin-defined holidays
- reminder engine
- snooze/repeat/windows notification settings per reminder
- advanced audit logging
- reports
- Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export
- backup and restore
- simple manual update model
- professional Windows 11 style UI
- friendly Persian messages

### Step 9 - Session Output Format

Every session response must include:

1. Project understanding summary
2. Detected weaknesses and risks
3. Clarification questions, if needed
4. Proposed next safe development step
5. One complete PowerShell script, only when enough information exists
6. Reminder of what file/report the user should send next time

Wait for user confirmation before moving to the next phase.


``n---



## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Dynamic Fields and Dynamic Reporting Added
- Added EntityFieldSetting for metadata-driven field activation and visibility.
- Added ReportDefinition, ReportColumn, ReportFilter, and ReportSort as the base model for dynamic reports.
- Added EmployeeOptionalFields as a safe optional-field pattern for future entity expansion.
- Introduced initial IEntityFieldSettingsService / EntityFieldSettingsService application service.
- The implementation follows the documented rule that settings and report metadata must remain database-driven and documentation must be updated automatically.

## 2026-06-15 ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ·ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¹ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹آ©ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ·ط·آ¢ط¢آ£ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط·آ¹أ¢â‚¬ع©ط·آ·ط¢آ¢ط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ·ط¢آ¥ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬أ¢â€‍آ¢ Dynamic Fields and Dynamic Reports
- Added EntityFieldSetting for metadata-driven field activation and visibility.
- Added ReportDefinition, ReportColumn, ReportFilter, and ReportSort as the dynamic report metadata model.
- Added EmployeeOptionalFields as a safe optional-field strategy.
- Added initial application service scaffold for field metadata.
- Documentation updated automatically by script, per project rules.

# Architecture Decisions ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ 2026-06-16

These decisions were confirmed during architecture clarification.

## Decision 001 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Database Naming Convention
Chosen Option: **d**

## Decision 002 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Domain Entity Strategy
Chosen Option: **b**

## Decision 003 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Audit Logging Strategy
Chosen Model: **Hybrid Audit**

Meaning:
- Critical actions logged in database
- Technical logs stored in file logs
- Security events duplicated for safety

## Decision 004 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Task Status Design
Implementation: **TaskStatus Table**

Reason:
Task status must be configurable and extensible instead of hard-coded enum.

## Decision 005 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reminder System
Architecture: **Generic Reminder Engine**

Capabilities:
- Works with multiple modules
- Supports snooze / repeat
- Supports multiple notification channels

## Decision 006 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Import Architecture
Strategy: **ImportService per module**

Example:
- EmployeeImportService
- DepartmentImportService
- TaskImportService

Advantages:
- Clear validation rules
- Module isolation
- Easier maintenance

## Decision 007 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Reporting Filters
Design:
**Dynamic Filter + Predefined Dataset**

Meaning:
- User-defined filters
- Prebuilt datasets for common reports
- Flexible reporting layer

## Decision 008 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Backup Configuration
Backup location controlled by:

**BackupLocation Setting**

Stored in:
System Settings table.

## Decision 009 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Architecture Choice
Chosen Option: **c**

## Decision 010 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Final Architecture Opinion

Recommended approach:

Hybrid enterprise desktop architecture combining:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- Metadata-driven reporting
- Dynamic field configuration
- Generic reminder engine
- RBAC security model

This ensures:

- maintainability
- scalability
- modular growth
- enterprise stability

## Decision 011 ط·آ·ط¢آ£ط·آ¢ط¢آ¢ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¹â€کط·آ¢ط¢آ¬ط·آ£ط¢آ¢ط£آ¢أ¢â‚¬ع‘ط¢آ¬ط£آ¢أ¢â€ڑآ¬ط¥â€™ Selected Option
Chosen Option: **a**

---


---


---

# EXTENSIBILITY ARCHITECTURE

## 1. Architectural Intent

The project supports an extensibility model centered on:

- metadata-driven reporting
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven settings
- controlled customization per organization
- maintainable core architecture

The purpose of this architecture is to allow the application to evolve without hardcoding all field and report behaviors into the UI or service layer.

---

## 2. Confirmed Dynamic Components

The roadmap already includes the following extensibility-related building blocks:

- EntityFieldSetting
- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

These components are used to support:

- field activation and visibility
- optional fields
- database-driven report definitions
- configurable report columns
- configurable report filters
- configurable report sorting

---

## 3. Customization Rules

Any customization must follow these rules:

1. **Database-driven first**
   - Settings and metadata must remain stored in the database where applicable.

2. **Core stability**
   - Core application logic must remain stable and avoid unnecessary hardcoding.

3. **Explicit field control**
   - Field visibility and activation must be handled through defined settings.

4. **Report configuration separation**
   - Report definitions must be independent from UI code as much as possible.

5. **Safe optional-field strategy**
   - Optional entity fields should follow the existing EmployeeOptionalFields approach when a structured extension is needed.

---

## 4. Dynamic Field Strategy

The project should use dynamic fields for cases where:

- a field may be enabled or disabled per deployment
- a field is optional for some organizations
- a field requires visibility control
- a field needs metadata-based behavior

The roadmap already confirms metadata-driven field activation and visibility, so this is the official direction for dynamic field behavior.

---

## 5. Dynamic Reporting Strategy

The reporting layer should remain metadata-driven.

That means reports can be defined using:

- a report definition
- selected columns
- filters
- sorting rules

This allows report behavior to be adjusted without rewriting the reporting infrastructure for every new requirement.

---

## 6. Field Settings Service

The presence of:

- IEntityFieldSettingsService
- EntityFieldSettingsService

confirms that the platform should expose a dedicated service layer for field metadata access and rule evaluation.

This service is responsible for reading and applying field configuration in a controlled way.

---

## 7. Extensibility Boundary

This extensibility model is **not** a plugin framework.

It is a controlled metadata-driven customization approach for:

- field behavior
- report behavior
- optional structured data
- settings-based runtime decisions

No plugin architecture is introduced by this document unless explicitly added later in the SSOT.

---

## 8. Resulting Design Direction

The confirmed design direction is:

- Modular Clean Monolith
- metadata-driven customization
- dynamic field configuration
- database-driven reporting metadata
- maintainable and enterprise-stable implementation

This keeps the system flexible while protecting the core codebase.

---

# ENTERPRISE DATABASE BLUEPRINT

## 1. Purpose

This section defines the official enterprise-grade database blueprint for the project.

The database must support:

- Modular Clean Monolith architecture
- WPF desktop deployment
- SQLite as the local relational database engine
- EF Core as the ORM
- RBAC security model
- auditability
- soft delete
- dynamic field configuration
- metadata-driven reporting
- reminder engine
- backup and restore
- maintainable future growth

This blueprint is part of the Single Source of Truth and must be used as the reference for all future entity, DbContext, migration, repository, and service implementations.

---

## 2. Database Design Rules

### 2.1 Common Entity Columns

Most business tables should include the following fields:

| Column | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Id | INTEGER / GUID | Yes | Primary key |
| CreatedAt | DateTime | Yes | Creation timestamp |
| CreatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who created the record |
| UpdatedAt | DateTime | No | Last update timestamp |
| UpdatedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who last updated the record |
| IsDeleted | Boolean | Yes | Soft delete flag |
| DeletedAt | DateTime | No | Delete timestamp |
| DeletedByUserId | INTEGER / GUID | No | User who deleted the record |
| RowVersion | INTEGER / BLOB | No | Concurrency control |

### 2.2 Soft Delete Rule

Business records should not be physically deleted by default.

Instead:

- IsDeleted = true
- DeletedAt is set
- DeletedByUserId is set when available

Hard delete is only allowed for temporary, cache, or system maintenance tables.

### 2.3 Audit Rule

Important business actions must be auditable.

Audit logs should capture:

- who performed the action
- what entity was affected
- what operation was performed
- when it happened
- previous values where practical
- new values where practical

### 2.4 Metadata Rule

Dynamic fields, dynamic reports, and configurable behavior must be database-driven.

Confirmed metadata-driven concepts include:

- EntityFieldSetting
- dynamic field activation
- dynamic field visibility
- ReportDefinition
- ReportColumn
- ReportFilter
- ReportSort
- EmployeeOptionalFields

### 2.5 SQLite Compatibility Rule

The schema must remain compatible with SQLite.

Avoid database features that are not portable or are difficult to maintain in SQLite, such as:

- stored procedures
- advanced server-side jobs
- database-specific procedural logic
- complex trigger-heavy behavior unless absolutely necessary

Business rules should primarily live in the application layer.

---

## 3. Table Groups Overview

The database is divided into the following logical groups:

1. Identity and Security
2. Organization and Application Settings
3. People and Business Entities
4. Documents and Attachments
5. Dynamic Fields and Metadata
6. Reporting
7. Reminder and Notification Engine
8. Audit and Activity Tracking
9. Backup and Maintenance
10. Import / Export
11. System Diagnostics

---

## 4. Identity and Security Tables

### 4.1 Users

Stores application users.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Username
- PasswordHash
- PasswordSalt
- DisplayName
- Email
- PhoneNumber
- IsActive
- IsSystemUser
- LastLoginAt
- FailedLoginCount
- LockoutEndAt
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId
- IsDeleted
- DeletedAt
- DeletedByUserId

### 4.2 Roles

Stores security roles.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- Name
- NormalizedName
- Description
- IsSystemRole
- IsActive
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId
- IsDeleted

### 4.3 Permissions

Stores granular permissions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- PermissionKey
- ModuleKey
- DisplayName
- Description
- IsSystemPermission
- CreatedAt
- UpdatedAt

Examples:

- Customers.View
- Customers.Create
- Customers.Update
- Customers.Delete
- Reports.View
- Reports.Export
- Settings.Manage
- Backup.Create
- Backup.Restore

### 4.4 UserRoles

Many-to-many relationship between users and roles.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- RoleId
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId

### 4.5 RolePermissions

Many-to-many relationship between roles and permissions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- RoleId
- PermissionId
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId

### 4.6 UserPermissionOverrides

Optional direct allow/deny permission overrides for a user.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- PermissionId
- IsAllowed
- Reason
- CreatedAt
- CreatedByUserId
- UpdatedAt
- UpdatedByUserId

### 4.7 UserSessions

Tracks login sessions.

Suggested columns:

- Id
- UserId
- LoginAt
- LogoutAt
- MachineName
- WindowsUsername
- AppVersion
- IpAddress
- IsActive

### 4.8 LoginHistory

Stores login attempts.

Suggested columns:

- Id

- UserId

- FailureReason
- AttemptedAt
- MachineName
- WindowsUsername
- AppVersion

## 15. Recommended Initial Implementation Order

Database implementation should be done in this order:

1. Base entity abstractions
2. Users
3. Roles
4. Permissions
5. UserRoles
6. RolePermissions
7. SystemSettings
8. OrganizationProfile
9. LookupCategories
10. LookupItems
11. Customers
12. Employees
13. EmployeeOptionalFields
14. EntityFieldSettings
15. ReportDefinitions
16. ReportColumns
17. ReportFilters
18. ReportSorts
19. AuditLogs
20. ErrorLogs
21. BackupSettings
22. BackupJobs
23. Reminders
24. Notifications

This sequence allows the system to bootstrap security, settings, domain data, metadata, reporting, logging, and backup gradually.

## 17. Implementation Notes

This blueprint is intentionally broader than the first development phase.

Not all tables must be implemented immediately.

The first implementation milestone should focus on:

- identity and security
- settings
- customers
- employees
- dynamic fields
- dynamic reports
- audit logs
- error logs
- backup settings

Additional tables should be implemented when their module becomes active.

---

## 18. SSOT Rule

If any future document, note, roadmap, audit, or implementation log conflicts with this database blueprint, this section in PROJECT_MASTER_ROADMAP.md takes precedence.
